issues.cause: Incorrectly defined alerting rules or thresholds lead to missed alerts or false positives.
issues.title: Alerting Rules Misconfigured
issues.rootReason: Human error during rule authoring, insufficient testing, or outdated configurations that do not reflect current system behavior.
issues.impactSummary: Critical incidents may go undetected, increasing mean time to detect (MTTD) and mean time to recovery (MTTR). Conversely, excessive false positives can lead to alert fatigue and desensitize on-call teams.
issues.recommendation: Implement a rigorous review process for all alerting rules. Utilize automated testing and validation frameworks to verify rule efficacy. Conduct regular audits of alert definitions against current operational needs.

issues.cause: The alerting engine cannot process the volume of incoming events or evaluate rules fast enough due to insufficient capacity.
issues.title: Alerting Engine Capacity Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Under-provisioning of instances or resources, or a sudden, unexpected spike in metric/log volume causing a backlog in rule evaluation.
issues.impactSummary: Delayed alert notifications for critical issues, potentially leading to increased downtime or service degradation. Some alerts may be dropped entirely, resulting in undetected failures.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the alerting engine instances or increase the capacity of existing instances. Implement auto-scaling based on CPU utilization, queue depth, or event processing rates. Optimize alerting rules for efficiency.

issues.cause: DNS Server is under runtime stress.
issues.title: DNS Server degradation
issues.rootReason: The DNS Server path is exhibiting degraded behavior.
issues.impactSummary: DNS Server degradation is affecting system reliability.
issues.recommendation: Review capacity, dependencies, and resilience settings for DNS Server.

assumptions: Fallback specialization generated because AI output was unavailable.

issues.impactSummary: NAT Gateway degradation is affecting system reliability.
issues.recommendation: Review capacity, dependencies, and resilience settings for NAT Gateway.

issues.title: Health Check Service degradation
issues.rootReason: The Health Check Service path is exhibiting degraded behavior.
issues.impactSummary: Health Check Service degradation is affecting system reliability.
issues.recommendation: Review capacity, dependencies, and resilience settings for Health Check Service.

issues.title: Rate Limiter degradation
issues.rootReason: The Rate Limiter path is exhibiting degraded behavior.
issues.impactSummary: Rate Limiter degradation is affecting system reliability.
issues.recommendation: Review capacity, dependencies, and resilience settings for Rate Limiter.

reviewWarnings: Review this auto-generated fallback draft in the admin studio.

issues.cause: Excessive requests from a client or overall traffic volume exceeding configured rate limits on the API Gateway.
issues.title: API Gateway Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Policy enforcement / Traffic management
issues.impactSummary: Clients receive 429 Too Many Requests, blocking access to downstream services.
issues.recommendation: Implement client-side rate limiting, adjust API Gateway rate limit policies, or scale API Gateway instances if the limit is per instance.

issues.cause: The API Gateway did not receive a timely response from an upstream service.
issues.title: Upstream Service Timeout (504 Gateway Timeout)
issues.rootReason: Upstream latency / Service unavailability
issues.impactSummary: API Gateway returns 504 Gateway Timeout errors to clients, indicating an issue with backend responsiveness.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the upstream service for performance bottlenecks, increase API Gateway timeout settings, or ensure upstream services are adequately scaled.

issues.cause: API Gateway health checks detected that one or more backend services are unhealthy and not responding correctly.
issues.title: Upstream Health Check Failure
issues.rootReason: Upstream service failure / Misconfiguration of health checks
issues.impactSummary: API Gateway stops routing traffic to unhealthy upstream instances, potentially reducing capacity or causing errors if all instances are unhealthy.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health of the upstream service, verify health check configuration, or adjust health check thresholds.

issues.cause: The API Gateway has exhausted its available connections to upstream services due to high concurrent requests or slow upstream responses.
issues.title: API Gateway Connection Pool Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Resource limits / Upstream congestion
issues.impactSummary: New requests to upstream services are queued or rejected by the API Gateway, leading to increased latency or errors for clients.
issues.recommendation: Increase API Gateway connection pool limits, optimize upstream service response times, or scale API Gateway instances.

issues.cause: Misconfigured routing rules, incorrect path matching, or unhandled routes causing requests to fail to reach their intended destination.
issues.title: API Gateway Routing Rule Mismatch
issues.rootReason: Configuration error / Policy mismatch
issues.impactSummary: Clients receive 404 Not Found or 500 Internal Server Error, indicating that the API Gateway cannot correctly route their requests.
issues.recommendation: Review and correct API Gateway routing configurations, ensure all necessary paths are covered, and validate deployment.

issues.cause: The API Gateway's circuit breaker has tripped for an upstream service, preventing further requests to that service due to a high error rate or timeout.
issues.title: API Gateway Circuit Breaker Open
issues.rootReason: Upstream service failure / Resiliency mechanism
issues.impactSummary: Requests to the affected upstream service are immediately failed by the API Gateway without attempting to reach the service, protecting the backend but causing client errors.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the root cause of upstream service failures, allow the circuit to reset, or manually reset it if appropriate.

issues.cause: The TLS certificate used by the API Gateway for client communication has expired, leading to failed HTTPS connections.
issues.title: API Gateway TLS Certificate Expired
issues.rootReason: Certificate management oversight / Security policy
issues.impactSummary: Clients are unable to establish secure connections, resulting in connection errors and complete service unavailability over HTTPS.
issues.recommendation: Renew and deploy a valid TLS certificate to the API Gateway.

assumptions: The API Gateway handles TLS termination, routing, and rate limiting.
The API Gateway performs health checks on its configured upstream services.
The API Gateway may employ circuit breakers for resiliency.

issues.cause: Redis not running or wrong host/port configured. The client cannot establish a TCP connection to the Redis server.
issues.title: ECONNREFUSED
issues.rootReason: Redis process crashed, was not started, or the client is pointing at the wrong address/port.
issues.impactSummary: All cache operations fail immediately. Application falls back to origin (database), causing load surge.
issues.recommendation: Verify Redis is running and listening on the expected host:port. Check firewall rules and security groups.

issues.cause: Connection dropped mid-operation due to a network blip, server restart, or TCP reset.
issues.title: ECONNRESET
issues.rootReason: Unstable network path, Redis server restarted, or an intermediate proxy closed the connection.
issues.impactSummary: In-flight commands are lost. Clients see transient errors until reconnection succeeds.
issues.recommendation: Enable automatic reconnection in the client. Use connection health checks and retry with backoff.

issues.cause: Connection or operation exceeded the configured timeout. Redis or the network is too slow to respond.
issues.title: ETIMEDOUT
issues.rootReason: Redis overloaded, slow network, or timeout configured too aggressively for the workload.
issues.impactSummary: Commands fail with timeout errors. Application latency spikes as it waits then falls back to origin.
issues.recommendation: Tune timeout values. Investigate Redis slowlog for long-running commands. Consider connection pooling.

issues.cause: DNS resolution failed for the Redis hostname. The client cannot resolve the server address.
issues.title: ENOTFOUND
issues.rootReason: DNS misconfiguration, hostname typo, or DNS infrastructure failure.
issues.impactSummary: Complete cache unavailability. All operations fail at the DNS resolution stage.
issues.recommendation: Verify hostname spelling and DNS configuration. Use IP addresses as fallback. Monitor DNS health.

issues.cause: Client closed connection before a response arrived. Usually caused by aggressive client-side timeouts.
issues.title: ECONNABORTED
issues.rootReason: Client timeout too short for the operation, or the client was shut down mid-request.
issues.impactSummary: Operations may complete on Redis but the client never sees the response. Risk of inconsistent state.
issues.recommendation: Align client timeout with expected operation duration. Use connection keep-alive settings.

issues.cause: Password not sent but Redis requires authentication. Client connected without AUTH.
issues.title: NOAUTH Authentication required
issues.rootReason: Redis configured with requirepass but client not configured with credentials.
issues.impactSummary: All commands fail after connection. Redis rejects every operation until AUTH is successful.
issues.recommendation: Configure client with the correct Redis password. Use ACL-based authentication for fine-grained access.

issues.cause: Wrong password, or AUTH sent when not required by the server.
issues.title: AUTH failed
issues.rootReason: Credential mismatch after rotation, copy-paste error, or AUTH sent to a server without requirepass.
issues.impactSummary: Client is connected but cannot execute any commands. Equivalent to full cache outage.
issues.recommendation: Verify credentials match. Implement credential rotation with zero-downtime swap. Check ACL configuration.

issues.cause: Client gave up retrying after too many consecutive connection failures.
issues.title: Max reconnection attempts reached
issues.rootReason: Extended Redis outage, persistent network issue, or reconnection backoff/limit too aggressive.
issues.impactSummary: Application permanently loses cache access until process restart or manual intervention.
issues.recommendation: Increase max retries or use exponential backoff with jitter. Implement a circuit breaker to avoid thundering herd on recovery.

issues.cause: Client lost connection and is in the process of retrying. Transient state during recovery.
issues.title: Connection lost – Attempting reconnect
issues.rootReason: Network blip, Redis restart, or keepalive timeout expired.
issues.impactSummary: Cache operations fail during reconnection window. Short-lived if Redis is healthy.
issues.recommendation: Monitor reconnection duration. If frequent, investigate underlying network stability or Redis health.

issues.cause: Calling a command designed for one data type on a key that holds a different type (e.g. LPUSH on a String key).
issues.title: WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value
issues.rootReason: Application logic bug, key naming collision, or schema migration left stale keys of the wrong type.
issues.impactSummary: The specific command fails. If unhandled, can cascade into application errors for the affected feature.
issues.recommendation: Use consistent key naming conventions with type prefixes. Add TYPE checks before operations. Clean up stale keys.

issues.cause: Missing or extra arguments passed to a Redis command.
issues.title: ERR wrong number of arguments
issues.rootReason: Application code bug, client library version mismatch, or incorrect command wrapper.
issues.impactSummary: Command is rejected. Feature relying on that command fails until code is fixed.
issues.recommendation: Fix the command invocation. Validate arguments before sending. Keep client library up to date.

issues.cause: Passing a float or string to INCR, EXPIRE, or similar commands that require integer arguments.
issues.title: ERR value is not an integer or out of range
issues.rootReason: Type coercion bug in application code, or stored value corrupted from a different code path.
issues.impactSummary: Atomic increment/decrement operations fail. Rate limiters, counters, or TTL management breaks.
issues.recommendation: Validate input types before sending commands. Use defensive parsing for values read from external sources.

issues.cause: Malformed command sent to the server. Redis cannot parse the command string.
issues.title: ERR syntax error
issues.rootReason: String interpolation bug, unsupported command on this Redis version, or protocol-level corruption.
issues.impactSummary: Command is rejected with no retry possible. Must be fixed in code.
issues.recommendation: Use a well-tested client library. Avoid raw command string construction. Validate Redis version compatibility.

issues.cause: Key does not exist. Usually safe but worth handling explicitly to avoid unexpected nil returns.
issues.title: ERR no such key
issues.rootReason: Key expired via TTL, was evicted under memory pressure, or was never set.
issues.impactSummary: Usually benign; becomes problematic if application treats missing keys as errors instead of cache misses.
issues.recommendation: Handle nil returns gracefully in application code. Use EXISTS check where needed. Monitor key eviction rates.

issues.cause: A command failed inside a MULTI block, aborting the entire transaction.
issues.title: EXECABORT Transaction discarded
issues.rootReason: One command in the transaction has a syntax or type error. Redis rejects the entire batch.
issues.impactSummary: Entire atomic transaction is rolled back. Multi-step cache operations left in an inconsistent state.
issues.recommendation: Validate all commands before MULTI/EXEC. Use WATCH for optimistic locking. Handle EXECABORT with retry logic.

issues.cause: EXEC called outside of a MULTI transaction block. Protocol-level logic error.
issues.title: EXEC without MULTI
issues.rootReason: State machine bug in transaction handling, or connection was reset mid-transaction.
issues.impactSummary: Command rejected. If connection dropped mid-MULTI, partial commands may have been queued and lost.
issues.recommendation: Ensure MULTI/EXEC pairs are atomic in your code. Handle connection reset by restarting the transaction.

issues.cause: Optimistic locking failed — the watched key was changed by another client between WATCH and EXEC.
issues.title: WATCH key was modified
issues.rootReason: High write contention on the watched key. Multiple clients competing to update the same data.
issues.impactSummary: Transaction is silently discarded (EXEC returns nil). Must be detected and retried by application code.
issues.recommendation: Implement retry logic for WATCH/MULTI/EXEC. Consider using Lua scripts for atomic operations instead.

issues.cause: Redis hit the maxmemory limit with noeviction policy set. New writes are rejected.
issues.title: OOM command not allowed when used memory > maxmemory
issues.rootReason: Insufficient memory allocation, no eviction policy configured, or unexpected data growth.
issues.impactSummary: All write commands fail. Read commands still work. Application cannot store new data in cache.
issues.recommendation: Set an appropriate maxmemory-policy (allkeys-lru or volatile-lru). Increase maxmemory or add shards.

issues.cause: OS-level memory fragmentation. Actual RSS is higher than the configured maxmemory limit.
issues.title: Used memory RSS exceeds maxmemory
issues.rootReason: Memory fragmentation from frequent allocation/deallocation patterns. Common with variable-size values.
issues.impactSummary: Risk of OS OOM killer terminating Redis. Performance degrades as OS starts swapping.
issues.recommendation: Run MEMORY PURGE. Consider a rolling restart. Use jemalloc allocator. Monitor mem_fragmentation_ratio.

issues.cause: Memory fragmentation is high. Redis is using significantly more RSS than the logical dataset size.
issues.title: High mem_fragmentation_ratio (>1.5)
issues.rootReason: Frequent key deletion and creation of different sizes. Allocator cannot efficiently reuse freed memory.
issues.impactSummary: Wasted memory reduces effective cache capacity. May trigger OOM before maxmemory is logically full.
issues.recommendation: Run MEMORY PURGE or schedule a rolling restart. Normalize value sizes where possible.

issues.cause: Eviction policy (LRU/LFU/volatile-*) is silently deleting keys to stay under maxmemory.
issues.title: maxmemory-policy evictions firing unexpectedly
issues.rootReason: Cache size too small for the working set. Or volatile-* policy evicting keys with TTL that should be retained.
issues.impactSummary: Cache hit rate drops as frequently-accessed keys are evicted. Backend load increases.
issues.recommendation: Increase maxmemory. Review eviction policy. Monitor evicted_keys metric. Consider splitting hot and cold data.

issues.cause: Running 32-bit Redis which has a hard 4 GB memory ceiling.
issues.title: WARNING: 32 bit instance detected
issues.rootReason: Legacy installation or container image using 32-bit Redis build.
issues.impactSummary: Cache capacity capped at 4 GB regardless of available system memory. Will hit OOM quickly under load.
issues.recommendation: Migrate to 64-bit Redis. All modern deployments should use 64-bit builds.

issues.cause: Background RDB save failed due to disk full, permission error, or I/O issue.
issues.title: BGSAVE failed
issues.rootReason: Insufficient disk space, wrong file permissions, or disk hardware failure.
issues.impactSummary: No point-in-time snapshot. Data at risk of loss on crash. Redis may block writes if stop-writes-on-bgsave-error is yes.
issues.recommendation: Check disk space and permissions. Monitor disk usage. Configure RDB save path on a reliable volume.

issues.cause: AOF compaction/rewrite failed. Check disk space and permissions.
issues.title: AOF rewrite failed
issues.rootReason: Disk full during rewrite, permission error, or background process killed.
issues.impactSummary: AOF file grows unbounded without compaction. Restart recovery time increases. Disk may fill up.
issues.recommendation: Free disk space. Check permissions on AOF directory. Monitor AOF file size growth.

issues.cause: OS lacks free memory to fork a child process for the RDB snapshot.
issues.title: Can't save in background: fork: Cannot allocate memory
issues.rootReason: Redis uses fork() for BGSAVE. The OS needs enough free memory for copy-on-write pages. overcommit_memory not set.
issues.impactSummary: No RDB snapshots can be created. Data durability is compromised. May also affect replication initial sync.
issues.recommendation: Set vm.overcommit_memory = 1. Ensure system has headroom for fork(). Consider using AOF-only persistence.

issues.cause: OS may kill child processes during RDB or AOF fork operations. Risk of failed snapshots.
issues.title: WARNING overcommit_memory is set to 0
issues.rootReason: Linux kernel setting vm.overcommit_memory defaults to 0 (heuristic), which may deny fork for large datasets.
issues.impactSummary: BGSAVE and BGREWRITEAOF may fail intermittently. Data persistence unreliable.
issues.recommendation: Set vm.overcommit_memory = 1 in sysctl. This is a standard Redis production hardening step.

issues.cause: AOF file path is misconfigured or a disk issue is preventing file creation.
issues.title: AOF enabled but no file opened
issues.rootReason: Non-existent directory in appendfilename config, or disk mount point failed.
issues.impactSummary: AOF is enabled but not recording. Complete data loss on crash, despite user believing persistence is active.
issues.recommendation: Verify appendfilename and dir configuration. Ensure the target directory exists and is writable.

issues.cause: Shutdown-time RDB snapshot could not be written. Data since last successful snapshot is lost.
issues.title: Saving the final RDB snapshot before exiting — failed
issues.rootReason: Disk full or permission error at shutdown time. Same root cause as BGSAVE failures.
issues.impactSummary: Data loss on restart. Magnitude depends on time since last successful persistence write.
issues.recommendation: Ensure disk space is available. Monitor BGSAVE success. Use AOF as a safety net.

issues.cause: Write command sent to a replica node. Must target the master.
issues.title: READONLY You can't write against a read only replica
issues.rootReason: Client routing error, failover not detected, or read/write splitting misconfigured.
issues.impactSummary: Write operations fail. If writes are critical-path, the feature is fully broken until routing is fixed.
issues.recommendation: Route write commands to the master. Use client-side write routing or a proxy like Redis Sentinel.

issues.cause: Replication backlog too small. A full resync was triggered instead of an incremental one.
issues.title: Partial resynchronization not possible
issues.rootReason: repl-backlog-size too small for the write throughput, or replica was disconnected too long.
issues.impactSummary: Full resync transfers the entire dataset, consuming bandwidth and CPU. Master may slow down during transfer.
issues.recommendation: Increase repl-backlog-size. Monitor replica offset drift. Reduce disconnect duration.

issues.cause: RDB persistence required but failing. Redis blocks writes to prevent data loss.
issues.title: MISCONF Redis is configured to save RDB snapshots but cannot persist
issues.rootReason: BGSAVE error combined with stop-writes-on-bgsave-error = yes (the default).
issues.impactSummary: All write operations rejected. Equivalent to a full write outage until persistence is restored.
issues.recommendation: Fix the underlying BGSAVE failure (disk space, permissions). Or set stop-writes-on-bgsave-error no (accepting risk).

issues.cause: Network or disk bottleneck is causing replication lag on the replica.
issues.title: Replica is lagging behind master
issues.rootReason: High write volume on master, slow replica disk, network congestion, or replica overloaded with reads.
issues.impactSummary: Reads from replica return stale data. Lag may grow unbounded if root cause is not addressed.
issues.recommendation: Monitor master_repl_offset vs replica offset. Reduce replica read load. Upgrade replica hardware.

issues.cause: Master rejected the replica during the handshake phase.
issues.title: Master aborted replication with an error
issues.rootReason: Version incompatibility, auth mismatch, or master under extreme memory pressure during BGSAVE for initial sync.
issues.impactSummary: Replica cannot sync. No redundancy until replication is re-established. Failover capability lost.
issues.recommendation: Check Redis version compatibility. Verify replica auth credentials. Ensure master has headroom for fork.

issues.cause: Key belongs to a different node. Client must redirect the request.
issues.title: MOVED <slot> <host>:<port>
issues.rootReason: Client not using cluster-aware mode, or slot mapping is stale after a rebalance.
issues.impactSummary: Extra round-trip latency per MOVED redirect. Clients without cluster support fail entirely.
issues.recommendation: Use a cluster-aware client library. Enable automatic redirection. Refresh slot mapping on MOVED errors.

issues.cause: Key is migrating to another node. Send ASKING before retrying on the target node.
issues.title: ASK <slot> <host>:<port>
issues.rootReason: Slot migration in progress during a cluster rebalance operation.
issues.impactSummary: Slight latency increase during migration. Transparent if client handles ASK correctly.
issues.recommendation: Handle ASK redirects in the client. Wait for migration to complete. Avoid large multi-key operations during rebalancing.

issues.cause: Not enough master nodes are reachable to maintain quorum.
issues.title: CLUSTERDOWN The cluster is down
issues.rootReason: Multiple master failures, network partition isolating masters, or cluster not properly initialized.
issues.impactSummary: Complete cache cluster outage. All operations fail until quorum is restored.
issues.recommendation: Ensure sufficient master+replica redundancy. Monitor cluster health. Use Redis Sentinel or managed Redis with auto-failover.

issues.cause: Multi-key operation spans different hash slots. Not allowed in cluster mode.
issues.title: CROSSSLOT Keys in request don't hash to the same slot
issues.rootReason: Application uses MGET/MSET or Lua scripts with keys that hash to different slots.
issues.impactSummary: Multi-key atomic operations fail. Must be redesigned for cluster compatibility.
issues.recommendation: Use hash tags ({prefix}) to colocate related keys in the same slot. Redesign multi-key operations.

issues.cause: Slot migration is in progress. Retry the request shortly.
issues.title: TRYAGAIN Multiple keys request during rehashing
issues.rootReason: Multi-key command hit while the slot is being migrated between nodes.
issues.impactSummary: Transient failures during cluster rebalancing. Self-resolves once migration completes.
issues.recommendation: Implement retry with short backoff. Avoid large multi-key operations during rebalancing.

issues.cause: Script not loaded on this server. Use EVALSHA after SCRIPT LOAD.
issues.title: NOSCRIPT No matching script
issues.rootReason: Redis restarted and lost cached scripts, or client is using EVALSHA without a SCRIPT LOAD fallback.
issues.impactSummary: Lua-script-dependent operations fail. Features using atomic Lua scripts are broken until scripts are reloaded.
issues.recommendation: Implement EVALSHA with EVAL fallback pattern. Re-load scripts on connection establishment.

issues.cause: A runtime error occurred inside the Lua script.
issues.title: ERR Error running script
issues.rootReason: Bug in Lua code, nil dereference, type mismatch, or exceeding script limits.
issues.impactSummary: The atomic operation fails. Partial state changes inside the script are rolled back by Redis.
issues.recommendation: Debug the Lua script locally. Add error handling within the script. Test with representative data.

issues.cause: A long-running Lua script is blocking the server. Use SCRIPT KILL to terminate.
issues.title: BUSY Redis is busy running a script
issues.rootReason: Lua script with O(n) operations on large datasets, infinite loops, or missing iteration limits.
issues.impactSummary: Entire Redis instance is blocked. All clients see timeouts. Equivalent to a full outage.
issues.recommendation: Set lua-time-limit. Break large operations into smaller scripts. Use SCRIPT KILL or SHUTDOWN NOSAVE in emergencies.

issues.cause: Cluster mode: Lua script accessed keys on different hash slots.
issues.title: Script attempted to access a non local key
issues.rootReason: Script KEYS argument contains keys from multiple slots. Not allowed in cluster mode.
issues.impactSummary: Script fails with error. Atomic operation cannot be performed across slots.
issues.recommendation: Use hash tags to colocate keys. Redesign scripts to operate on keys within a single slot.

issues.cause: Sentinel detected master as subjectively or objectively down.
issues.title: +sdown / +odown
issues.rootReason: Master crashed, network partition, or master too slow to respond to PING within sentinel down-after-milliseconds.
issues.impactSummary: Triggers failover process. Writes may be rejected during failover window (typically 5-30 seconds).
issues.recommendation: Tune down-after-milliseconds. Ensure Redis health checks are responsive. Monitor sentinel logs.

issues.cause: A replica is being promoted to master by Sentinel.
issues.title: Failover in progress
issues.rootReason: Master failure detected and quorum agreed on failover. Normal HA behavior.
issues.impactSummary: Brief write outage during promotion (5-30 seconds). Reads from replicas may continue.
issues.recommendation: Monitor failover duration. Ensure clients re-discover the new master via Sentinel. Use connection retry logic.

issues.cause: Quorum not met. Failover cannot proceed without enough Sentinel votes.
issues.title: Not enough sentinels
issues.rootReason: Too few Sentinel instances running, or network partition isolating Sentinels from each other.
issues.impactSummary: Automatic failover is disabled. If master fails, manual intervention is required for recovery.
issues.recommendation: Deploy at least 3 Sentinel instances across failure domains. Monitor Sentinel process health.

issues.cause: Network partition or master crash detected by Sentinel.
issues.title: Sentinel can't connect to master
issues.rootReason: Firewall rules changed, master process died, or network path between Sentinel and master is broken.
issues.impactSummary: Sentinel cannot monitor master health. If master is actually down, failover will be delayed or blocked.
issues.recommendation: Verify network connectivity between Sentinel and master. Check firewall rules. Review master process health.

issues.cause: Redis returned an error response to a client command.
issues.title: ReplyError
issues.rootReason: Command-level error: wrong arguments, type mismatch, permission denied, or server-side rejection.
issues.impactSummary: Individual command fails. Impact depends on whether the error is caught and handled by the application.
issues.recommendation: Inspect the error message for the specific Redis error code. Fix the offending command.

issues.cause: Command was aborted because the connection was lost mid-flight.
issues.title: AbortError
issues.rootReason: Network failure, Redis restart, or client-side timeout killed the connection while a command was pending.
issues.impactSummary: Pending commands are lost. Application must detect and retry or fail gracefully.
issues.recommendation: Implement retry logic with idempotency. Use connection pooling with health checks.

issues.cause: The client library gave up retrying a failed command (e.g. ioredis maxRetriesPerRequest).
issues.title: MaxRetriesPerRequestError
issues.rootReason: Sustained Redis unavailability exceeding the configured retry budget.
issues.impactSummary: Application code receives unrecoverable error. Cache-dependent features fail.
issues.recommendation: Increase maxRetriesPerRequest or implement application-level fallback. Add circuit breaker to prevent retry storms.

issues.cause: One or more commands in a pipelined batch failed.
issues.title: Pipeline error
issues.rootReason: Network error during pipeline flush, or individual command errors within the batch.
issues.impactSummary: Partial batch failure. Some commands may have succeeded while others failed. Requires per-command error inspection.
issues.recommendation: Handle individual command errors within pipeline results. Use MULTI/EXEC for atomic batches.

issues.cause: The underlying TCP socket closed while a response was being streamed.
issues.title: Socket closed unexpectedly
issues.rootReason: Redis crashed, was killed by OOM, or a network device reset the connection.
issues.impactSummary: All in-flight commands on this connection are lost. Client must reconnect and retry.
issues.recommendation: Implement automatic reconnection. Monitor Redis process stability. Check for OOM kills in system logs.

assumptions: Seeded from the existing hand-written cache specialization catalog.
reviewWarnings: Review component-level applicability before shipping runtime behavior changes.

issues.cause: The replica database is falling behind the primary due to high write volume, network latency, or inefficient replication processes.
issues.title: Database Replication Lag
issues.rootReason: High write throughput or network latency preventing replica synchronization with the primary.
issues.impactSummary: Reads from replicas may return stale data, affecting application consistency and potentially leading to data loss if a failover occurs while the replica is significantly behind.
issues.recommendation: Optimize write queries, increase replica capacity, improve network connectivity between primary and replicas, or consider adjusting replication configuration for better throughput.

issues.cause: The database has reached its maximum allowed concurrent connections, preventing new connections from being established.
issues.title: Database Connection Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Insufficient database connection capacity to handle demand from connected applications.
issues.impactSummary: Applications attempting to connect to the database will receive connection errors or timeouts, leading to service unavailability or degraded performance for dependent services.
issues.recommendation: Increase the database's maximum connection limit (if possible), optimize application connection pooling, identify and fix connection leaks, or scale database instances.

issues.cause: A single partition within a sharded database cluster is receiving a disproportionately high volume of read or write traffic compared to other partitions.
issues.title: Database Hot Partition
issues.rootReason: Uneven distribution of data or access patterns due to an ineffective sharding key or highly concentrated workload.
issues.impactSummary: Performance degradation for queries targeting the hot partition, increased latency, and potential unavailability for data stored on that partition, even if other partitions are healthy.
issues.recommendation: Re-evaluate and adjust the sharding key, redistribute data across partitions, or consider scaling resources specifically for the overloaded partition.

issues.cause: The underlying disk subsystem is unable to handle the volume of read and write operations generated by the database, leading to I/O queues and increased latency.
issues.title: Database Disk I/O Saturation
issues.rootReason: Insufficient disk throughput capacity for the current database workload or inefficient query patterns.
issues.impactSummary: Significant increase in query execution times, overall database operations slow down, and the entire system's performance degrades due to I/O bottlenecks.
issues.recommendation: Optimize slow queries, add appropriate indexes, provision faster storage with higher IOPS/throughput, or distribute read loads across replicas to reduce I/O on the primary.

assumptions: Database components are configured with default replication and sharding settings which imply topology-aware failure scenarios.
Monitoring metrics for replication lag, connection counts, partition activity, and disk I/O are available and accurate.

issues.cause: TLS negotiation fails.
issues.title: SSL Handshake Failure
issues.rootReason: Invalid or expired certificate, or a protocol mismatch between client and load balancer.
issues.impactSummary: Client requests are being rejected during the TLS handshake before any backend can receive them.
issues.recommendation: Renew certificates, align TLS versions, and verify TLS termination settings.

issues.cause: Backend hostname cannot be resolved.
issues.title: DNS Resolution Failure
issues.rootReason: DNS or service-discovery configuration is misaligned, so the load balancer cannot resolve backend endpoints.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic cannot be routed to backend hostnames, so edge requests stall or fail before delivery.
issues.recommendation: Fix DNS records or service-registry wiring and validate service discovery health.

issues.cause: No healthy backends are available.
issues.title: 503 Service Unavailable
issues.rootReason: All backend instances failed health checks or are still scaling/warming up.
issues.impactSummary: The backend pool is empty from the load balancer perspective, so edge traffic is being dropped instead of forwarded.
issues.recommendation: Fix health checks, add healthy instances, and tune autoscaling so capacity is ready sooner.

issues.cause: Backend is not accepting connections.
issues.title: Connection Refused
issues.rootReason: The backend service is down, not listening on the expected port, or has no ready instances.
issues.impactSummary: Forwarded requests are failing at connect time, so the load balancer drops traffic before it can reach an accepting backend.
issues.recommendation: Start the service, verify the listening port, and confirm the target is ready before routing traffic.

issues.cause: Backend closed the connection mid-flight.
issues.title: Connection Reset
issues.rootReason: The backend is unstable or overloaded and is resetting sockets before requests complete.
issues.impactSummary: Some forwarded requests are being torn down mid-flight, which turns accepted edge traffic into backend failures.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the backend, reduce overload, and raise safe connection limits where appropriate.

issues.cause: Backend response exceeds the load balancer request timeout.
issues.title: 504 Gateway Timeout
issues.rootReason: Long-running backend work, database slowness, or thread exhaustion is pushing responses past the LB timeout budget.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are spending too long in the backend path and are timing out before a usable response returns.
issues.recommendation: Optimize slow queries, raise the request timeout carefully, or scale backend capacity.

issues.cause: The load balancer dropped a long-running request.
issues.title: Idle Timeout
issues.rootReason: The LB idle timeout is shorter than backend processing time for this path.
issues.impactSummary: Long-running requests are abandoned at the edge while the backend path is still in flight.
issues.recommendation: Increase the LB idle timeout or reduce backend processing time on the slow path.

issues.cause: Targets are being marked unhealthy.
issues.title: Health Check Failure
issues.rootReason: Health checks are disabled, misconfigured, or backend dependencies are failing on the health endpoint.
issues.impactSummary: Probe results are pulling healthy capacity out of rotation, reducing how much traffic the LB can safely deliver.
issues.recommendation: Implement a lightweight /health endpoint, verify the path and thresholds, and raise the timeout if probes are too strict.

issues.cause: Rate limiting is shedding traffic.
issues.title: 429 Too Many Requests
issues.rootReason: Traffic spikes or overly strict rate rules are rejecting requests before they reach the backend.
issues.impactSummary: The load balancer is shedding excess traffic before it reaches the backend pool.
issues.recommendation: Raise safe limits, add upstream throttling strategy, or absorb bursts with caching and capacity.

issues.cause: The load balancer cannot complete a valid backend connection.
issues.title: 502 Bad Gateway
issues.rootReason: A backend crashed, the wrong port is configured, or a network/security rule is blocking the path.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are accepted at the edge but cannot be completed into a valid upstream response.
issues.recommendation: Verify the backend service is running, confirm port mappings, and check firewall or security-group rules.

issues.cause: Some backend instances are unhealthy.
issues.title: Partial Target Failure
issues.rootReason: A subset of instances failed health checks or never became ready after deployment/scale-out.
issues.impactSummary: Part of the backend pool is unavailable, so the remaining targets absorb the surviving traffic.
issues.recommendation: Replace unhealthy nodes and harden readiness checks so bad instances leave rotation faster.

issues.cause: Backend responses are slow.
issues.title: High Backend Latency
issues.rootReason: Resource contention or inefficient backend code is inflating response time without fully timing out yet.
issues.impactSummary: A large share of routed traffic is still completing, but it is spending significantly longer in backend execution.
issues.recommendation: Optimize backend hotspots, scale serving capacity, and use caching where it meaningfully cuts response time.

issues.cause: The LB is running out of backend connections.
issues.title: Connection Pool Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Concurrent request volume is exhausting the available backend connection pool before a full outage is visible.
issues.impactSummary: Connection headroom is nearly exhausted, so a growing slice of traffic is at risk of queueing or being dropped.
issues.recommendation: Increase connection pool size or scale backend capacity so concurrency is spread across more targets.

issues.cause: Traffic is imbalanced across targets.
issues.title: Uneven Load Distribution
issues.rootReason: Sticky sessions or an imbalanced hashing/routing strategy is sending too much traffic to the hottest backend.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic is concentrating on the hottest backend instead of being shared evenly across the healthy pool.
issues.recommendation: Adjust the balancing algorithm, reduce stickiness, or smooth hashing so traffic spreads more evenly.

issues.cause: New instances are getting traffic too early.
issues.title: Slow Start Issues
issues.rootReason: Cold-starting instances are receiving live traffic before they have fully warmed up.
issues.impactSummary: Cold backends are receiving live traffic too early, which reduces successful delivery while they warm up.
issues.recommendation: Add more warm-up time and delay live routing until new instances are ready.

issues.cause: Weak or outdated TLS is still allowed.
issues.title: TLS Degradation
issues.rootReason: The load balancer is configured to accept legacy TLS, or clients are falling back to older negotiated versions.
issues.impactSummary: Legacy TLS policy is broadening the risky client population and increasing the chance of degraded edge handshakes.
issues.recommendation: Enforce modern TLS minimums and update client/server TLS configuration.

issues.cause: Retry volume is rising sharply.
issues.title: Retry Spike
issues.rootReason: Backend instability is forcing callers to retry into an already unhealthy path.
issues.impactSummary: Retries are adding extra attempted load on top of already unstable backend traffic.
issues.recommendation: Reduce retry pressure, add backoff/circuit breaking, and fix the backend instability driving retries.

issues.cause: Request headers exceed the configured limit.
issues.title: Header Too Large
issues.rootReason: Large cookies, auth headers, or feature-flag metadata are inflating request headers beyond the LB limit.
issues.impactSummary: Oversized headers are being rejected at the edge before the request can enter the backend path.
issues.recommendation: Reduce cookie/header size or raise the limit only if the upstream path can safely support it.

issues.cause: Targets keep switching health state.
issues.title: Health Check Flapping
issues.rootReason: Health thresholds are too tight or a backend dependency is unstable enough to make readiness oscillate.
issues.impactSummary: Backend targets are oscillating in and out of rotation, which churns where live traffic can be sent.
issues.recommendation: Relax health thresholds slightly and stabilize the dependency or startup path causing the oscillation.

issues.cause: Listener is forwarding to an empty target group.
issues.title: Target Group Empty
issues.rootReason: No backend targets are registered behind the load balancer rule.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are matching the load balancer but there is no backend pool available to receive them.
issues.recommendation: Register healthy targets or reconnect the listener rule to a populated target group.

issues.cause: Every target has fallen out of rotation.
issues.title: All Targets Deregistered
issues.rootReason: Health checks or control-plane state have removed the full backend pool.
issues.impactSummary: No backend target remains eligible for routing, so the LB is dropping edge traffic outright.
issues.recommendation: Restore healthy targets and verify the target registration workflow is not draining the full pool.

issues.cause: The configured health probe path is invalid.
issues.title: Wrong Health Path
issues.rootReason: The load balancer is probing an endpoint that does not exist or cannot return healthy status.
issues.impactSummary: Targets are being marked unhealthy because the probe path itself is wrong.
issues.recommendation: Point the load balancer to a valid lightweight health endpoint like `/health`.

issues.cause: Health probes are timing out before the backend responds.
issues.title: Health Check Timeout
issues.rootReason: The health-check timeout is tighter than the backend response time under current load.
issues.impactSummary: Probe timeouts are shrinking the healthy pool even when the backend might still serve real traffic.
issues.recommendation: Raise the health-check timeout or make the probe path lighter and faster.

issues.cause: The health probe is speaking the wrong protocol.
issues.title: Protocol Mismatch on Probe
issues.rootReason: The load balancer is probing the backend with an HTTP/TLS protocol that the target does not accept.
issues.impactSummary: Targets fail readiness because the health probe handshake itself is invalid.
issues.recommendation: Align the health-check protocol with the backend listener and TLS settings.

issues.cause: Health probes are using the wrong backend port.
issues.title: Probe Port Mismatch
issues.rootReason: The health-check port is misconfigured and does not reach the real service listener.
issues.impactSummary: Targets are drained from rotation because the probe never reaches the correct backend port.
issues.recommendation: Correct the probe port and validate the backend listener mapping.

issues.cause: Health thresholds are too aggressive.
issues.title: Threshold Too Low
issues.rootReason: A single transient failure is enough to churn targets in and out of rotation.
issues.impactSummary: Overly tight thresholds increase routing churn and make more traffic bounce across the pool.
issues.recommendation: Increase the healthy/unhealthy thresholds so brief blips do not trigger load-balancer churn.

issues.cause: A backend target is unhealthy.
issues.title: Target Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: One or more targets are failing readiness checks or backend dependencies.
issues.impactSummary: Unhealthy targets reduce the available backend pool and shift more traffic to the survivors.
issues.recommendation: Repair the unhealthy target and verify the readiness path and dependency health.

issues.cause: A target keeps bouncing between healthy and unhealthy.
issues.title: Flapping Target
issues.rootReason: Readiness is unstable or health thresholds are too strict for the backend behavior.
issues.impactSummary: Routing keeps shifting as the target oscillates in and out of the healthy pool.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the dependency path and relax the probe threshold enough to absorb transient blips.

issues.cause: The load balancer cannot establish a backend connection in time.
issues.title: Connection Timeout
issues.rootReason: Routing, security, or network-path issues are causing backend connects to time out instead of completing quickly.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are accepted at the edge but stall while the LB waits for backend connects that never complete.
issues.recommendation: Validate routing, ACLs, and network reachability between the load balancer and its targets.

issues.cause: The load balancer cannot reach the upstream host.
issues.title: Upstream Connect Error
issues.rootReason: A firewall, bad IP, or network partition is dropping backend connects without a clean socket failure.
issues.impactSummary: Backend connects are failing silently on the path, so accepted edge traffic never reaches a live upstream.
issues.recommendation: Check target IPs, firewalls, and network-path reachability to the backend host.

issues.cause: The load balancer reused a stale backend connection.
issues.title: Keep-Alive Stale Connection
issues.rootReason: The backend closed a keep-alive socket that the load balancer still believed was reusable.
issues.impactSummary: A slice of reused backend connections is stale, turning otherwise routable requests into immediate failures.
issues.recommendation: Set the LB keep-alive timeout slightly below the backend keep-alive timeout and reduce stale connection reuse.

issues.cause: One side believes the connection is still open while the other does not.
issues.title: Half-Open TCP Connection
issues.rootReason: Network instability is leaving some TCP sessions in a half-open state until the next request fails.
issues.impactSummary: Some connection state is stale or one-sided, causing intermittent request failures and retries.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the network path and tighten idle and keep-alive coordination across the LB and backend.

issues.cause: The load balancer is running out of source NAT tuples.
issues.title: Source IP Exhaustion (SNAT)
issues.rootReason: A very high connection rate to the same backend path is exhausting ephemeral source IP and port combinations.
issues.impactSummary: New backend connects are failing because the edge no longer has enough source NAT capacity for the traffic pattern.
issues.recommendation: Spread traffic across more targets or NAT capacity and reduce connection churn on the backend path.

issues.cause: Traffic to another zone or region is failing.
issues.title: Cross-Zone Routing Failure
issues.rootReason: Cross-zone connectivity is degraded or the load balancer is not fully wired to the target zones it depends on.
issues.impactSummary: Cross-zone backend capacity is unavailable, forcing traffic into a smaller and less resilient slice of the pool.
issues.recommendation: Enable the required zones and validate cross-zone routing and firewall rules.

issues.cause: The load balancer surge queue is filling faster than backends can drain it.
issues.title: Backpressure / Surge Queue Full
issues.rootReason: Incoming request rate is outpacing downstream processing capacity, so queued requests are being dropped.
issues.impactSummary: The edge queue is saturating, so some requests are dropped before backend service begins.
issues.recommendation: Add backend capacity, reduce burst pressure, or protect the edge earlier with throttling and caching.

issues.cause: Long-lived upgraded connections are not staying open long enough.
issues.title: WebSocket Upgrade Failure
issues.rootReason: Idle timeout and protocol handling are too strict for upgrade-style traffic like WebSockets or SSE.
issues.impactSummary: Long-lived upgraded connections are failing prematurely, dropping real-time traffic at the edge.
issues.recommendation: Increase idle timeout and verify the listener path supports upgrade semantics end to end.

issues.cause: The presented TLS certificate is expired.
issues.title: Certificate Expired
issues.rootReason: The certificate renewal path failed or the certificate metadata is stale.
issues.impactSummary: TLS sessions are failing immediately because clients reject the expired certificate.
issues.recommendation: Renew the certificate and verify the automated renewal path before expiry.

issues.cause: The hostname does not match the certificate SANs.
issues.title: Certificate CN / SAN Mismatch
issues.rootReason: The requested hostname is missing from the certificate subject alternative names.
issues.impactSummary: TLS handshakes are failing because the certificate identity does not match the requested host.
issues.recommendation: Reissue the certificate with the correct SANs or route traffic through the matching hostname.

issues.cause: Intermediate certificates are missing.
issues.title: Incomplete Certificate Chain
issues.rootReason: The certificate chain being served by the load balancer is incomplete.
issues.impactSummary: TLS validation is failing on clients that do not already cache the missing intermediate chain.
issues.recommendation: Serve the full certificate chain, not just the leaf certificate.

issues.cause: Server name indication is not configured for the requested host.
issues.title: SNI Not Configured
issues.rootReason: The listener is not selecting the correct certificate for the requested hostname.
issues.impactSummary: Hostname-based TLS traffic is failing because the listener cannot choose the correct certificate.
issues.recommendation: Configure SNI on the listener and attach the correct certificate mapping for each hostname.

issues.cause: The certificate and private key do not match.
issues.title: Private Key Mismatch
issues.rootReason: A broken certificate deployment paired the wrong key material with the active certificate.
issues.impactSummary: TLS sessions fail at the edge because the listener cannot prove ownership of the served certificate.
issues.recommendation: Redeploy the matching certificate and private key pair to the listener.

issues.cause: The load balancer is not serving valid OCSP stapling data.
issues.title: OCSP Stapling Failure
issues.rootReason: Certificate status data is stale or missing during TLS negotiation.
issues.impactSummary: Some clients may soft-fail or slow down TLS negotiation while certificate revocation data is unavailable.
issues.recommendation: Repair OCSP stapling on the certificate path and verify revocation status freshness.

issues.cause: TLS validation to the backend is failing.
issues.title: Backend TLS Verification Failure
issues.rootReason: The upstream TLS policy or trust settings do not match what the backend presents.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream TLS verification is blocking otherwise routable requests from completing.
issues.recommendation: Align backend TLS policy, trust roots, and protocol settings across the LB and target service.

issues.cause: Legacy or weak TLS ciphers are still enabled.
issues.title: Weak Cipher Suite
issues.rootReason: The TLS policy is broad enough to accept weaker cryptographic configurations than modern clients require.
issues.impactSummary: Weak TLS policy expands the set of risky handshakes and increases exposure to degraded secure transport.
issues.recommendation: Tighten the TLS policy to modern cipher suites and TLS 1.2+ only.

issues.cause: Abusive TLS renegotiation is amplifying edge work.
issues.title: TLS Renegotiation Attack
issues.rootReason: Legacy TLS posture is allowing a malicious traffic pattern to spend too much work in the handshake path.
issues.impactSummary: Edge CPU and handshake capacity are being consumed by abusive renegotiation rather than productive requests.
issues.recommendation: Disable unsafe renegotiation behavior and tighten TLS policy for abusive clients.

issues.cause: The load balancer is holding a large number of active backend connections.
issues.title: High Active Connection Count
issues.rootReason: Connection concurrency is climbing toward saturation under the current traffic mix.
issues.impactSummary: High connection concurrency is reducing headroom for the next burst or reconnect storm.
issues.recommendation: Add backend capacity or connection headroom before the pool turns into a hard limit.

issues.cause: The edge is returning too many 5xx responses.
issues.title: Elevated 5xx Rate
issues.rootReason: Upstream failures and timeouts are consuming a significant share of requests.
issues.impactSummary: A meaningful slice of edge traffic is failing with server-side errors instead of completing successfully.
issues.recommendation: Reduce upstream error pressure before the service burns through more user-visible error budget.

issues.cause: Latency spiked across the target group.
issues.title: Latency Spike on Target Group
issues.rootReason: Backend work is slowing down enough to create a visible target-group latency spike before full timeout.
issues.impactSummary: The target group is still serving traffic, but response time has spiked enough to degrade user experience.
issues.recommendation: Inspect backend hotspots and scale the target group before the latency spike becomes a timeout incident.

issues.cause: Traffic is not balanced evenly across zones.
issues.title: Cross-Zone Imbalance
issues.rootReason: Zone-level capacity or routing coverage is uneven, so one zone is carrying too much of the load.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic is concentrating in fewer zones than intended, reducing resilience and increasing hot-spot risk.
issues.recommendation: Restore zonal symmetry so the load balancer can spread traffic evenly across all intended zones.

issues.cause: The access-log pipeline is not keeping up.
issues.title: Access Log Write Failure
issues.rootReason: Observability backpressure is preventing the load balancer from writing access logs reliably.
issues.impactSummary: Operational visibility is degraded because access logs are not being delivered reliably.
issues.recommendation: Restore log pipeline throughput so access logs can be emitted without backpressure.

issues.cause: The load balancer is holding onto stale backend addresses.
issues.title: Stale DNS / IP Pinning
issues.rootReason: Dynamic discovery updates are not propagating cleanly, so some traffic still pins to old backend endpoints.
issues.impactSummary: Part of the traffic is still pinned to stale backend destinations, reducing usable delivery.
issues.recommendation: Refresh DNS or service-discovery propagation and lower stale endpoint lifetime on the LB.

issues.cause: Header forwarding behavior is inconsistent.
issues.title: Header Injection / Forwarding Gap
issues.rootReason: Header propagation or sanitation rules are drifting enough to produce broken or unsafe downstream requests.
issues.impactSummary: Header handling drift is causing request compatibility issues and avoidable edge rejects.
issues.recommendation: Normalize forwarded-header policy and sanitize unexpected edge headers before routing downstream.

issues.cause: Response compression and payload handling are conflicting.
issues.title: Response Compression Conflict
issues.rootReason: Large payload behavior is creating buffering and latency side effects on the edge path.
issues.impactSummary: Large payload handling is increasing edge buffering and delaying response delivery.
issues.recommendation: Revisit compression policy and response size so the edge does not spend disproportionate time buffering.

issues.cause: Control-plane changes have not propagated evenly.
issues.title: Config Propagation Delay
issues.rootReason: Some edge instances are serving newer LB config while others still serve the old state.
issues.impactSummary: Mixed edge behavior is producing request inconsistency while the new configuration converges.
issues.recommendation: Let control-plane rollout finish or slow the change cadence so edge config converges cleanly.

issues.cause: Metrics export from the edge is delayed or missing.
issues.title: Metrics / CloudWatch Gap
issues.rootReason: Observability backpressure is creating blind spots in edge monitoring and cloud metrics.
issues.impactSummary: Monitoring visibility is degraded, increasing detection and debugging latency for edge issues.
issues.recommendation: Restore metrics pipeline capacity so edge telemetry remains trustworthy during incidents.

issues.cause: The load balancer is hitting an edge throughput boundary.
issues.title: Throughput Limit Hit
issues.rootReason: The current traffic burst is larger than the load balancer can safely deliver without throttling or queueing.
issues.impactSummary: Delivered throughput is capped, so excess edge traffic is being slowed, queued, or dropped.
issues.recommendation: Pre-scale edge and backend capacity or trim burst pressure before limits are hit.

issues.cause: Incoming requests are not matching a listener rule.
issues.title: No Listener Rule Match
issues.rootReason: Routing conditions are too narrow or misordered, so valid requests do not match a backend forwarding rule.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are rejected at the edge before any backend is selected because no listener rule matches them.
issues.recommendation: Fix host/path/header rules and verify listener precedence so valid traffic has a route.

issues.cause: Traffic is arriving on a listener with the wrong protocol expectations.
issues.title: Wrong Listener Protocol
issues.rootReason: HTTP and TLS listener configuration are misaligned with the actual traffic reaching the load balancer.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are being rejected at the listener before they can enter the normal backend routing path.
issues.recommendation: Align listener protocol and port settings with the traffic the edge actually receives.

issues.cause: The edge is not redirecting plain HTTP to HTTPS.
issues.title: Missing HTTP-to-HTTPS Redirect
issues.rootReason: Listener configuration drift has left insecure HTTP paths enabled without the expected redirect policy.
issues.impactSummary: A portion of traffic can remain on a less secure path instead of being upgraded to HTTPS at the edge.
issues.recommendation: Add or restore an HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect rule on the load balancer.

issues.cause: Network policy is blocking the LB or backend port.
issues.title: Incorrect Security Group
issues.rootReason: Security-group or firewall rules are preventing traffic from traversing the expected listener or backend path.
issues.impactSummary: Network policy is silently blocking requests, turning routed traffic into hard backend delivery failures.
issues.recommendation: Open the required ports and allow the load balancer to reach the backend security group.

issues.cause: The load balancer is not enabled in every target zone.
issues.title: Subnet / AZ Not Enabled
issues.rootReason: Some backend regions or zones are outside the load balancer coverage area.
issues.impactSummary: Part of the intended backend capacity is unusable because the LB is not present in every required zone.
issues.recommendation: Enable the missing zone or subnet coverage so all intended targets are reachable.

issues.cause: Listener rule evaluation order is wrong.
issues.title: Rule Priority Conflict
issues.rootReason: A broader rule is winning before a more specific one, diverting traffic to the wrong action.
issues.impactSummary: A share of edge traffic is being misrouted because rule priority no longer reflects route specificity.
issues.recommendation: Reorder listener rules so the most specific rule matches before generic catch-all routes.

issues.cause: The load balancer is routing to the wrong backend port.
issues.title: Incorrect Backend Port
issues.rootReason: Listener-to-target port mapping does not match the real backend service listener.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are accepted at the edge but fail immediately because the LB is dialing the wrong backend port.
issues.recommendation: Fix the target port mapping and verify the backend listener is open on that port.

issues.cause: Sticky-session cookie state no longer lines up with the target pool.
issues.title: Sticky Session Cookie Mismatch
issues.rootReason: Cookie affinity is pinning requests to stale or uneven backend assignments.
issues.impactSummary: Affinity cookies are concentrating traffic and sending a slice of requests toward the wrong backend state.
issues.recommendation: Refresh sticky-session policy and reduce stale cookie affinity after target changes.

issues.cause: The edge is being pressured by a connection-flood pattern.
issues.title: SYN Flood
issues.rootReason: Abusive connection attempts are forcing the load balancer to spend capacity on connection handling instead of productive traffic.
issues.impactSummary: Connection-flood traffic is stealing edge capacity from legitimate requests.
issues.recommendation: Enable upstream filtering and absorb the flood before it reaches listener capacity.

issues.cause: The edge is receiving a burst of abusive HTTP traffic.
issues.title: HTTP Flood
issues.rootReason: Flood traffic volume is high enough that the load balancer is shedding or starving legitimate requests.
issues.impactSummary: Flood traffic is consuming the edge request budget and causing the LB to drop legitimate work.
issues.recommendation: Add filtering, caching, or upstream shielding so flood traffic is absorbed before the LB saturates.

issues.cause: Slow client behavior is tying up edge connections.
issues.title: Slow Loris Attack
issues.rootReason: Long-lived partially idle connections are consuming listener capacity faster than useful work can complete.
issues.impactSummary: Connection slots are staying busy without completing useful work, reducing throughput for healthy requests.
issues.recommendation: Tighten slow-client protection and reduce idle hold time for abusive connection patterns.

issues.cause: Edge policy is suddenly blocking far more requests.
issues.title: WAF Rule Block Spike
issues.rootReason: Traffic shape and edge policy drift are combining to reject an unusually large volume of requests.
issues.impactSummary: A rising fraction of traffic is being rejected at the edge policy layer before backend routing begins.
issues.recommendation: Review WAF and edge policy changes so legitimate traffic is not overblocked during the spike.

issues.cause: The edge is returning an unusual number of 4xx responses.
issues.title: Anomalous 4xx Rate Spike
issues.rootReason: Header rejects, throttling, or routing mismatches are causing more client-facing edge rejections than normal.
issues.impactSummary: A noticeable share of traffic is being rejected at the edge instead of reaching the backend path.
issues.recommendation: Reduce false rejects and edge throttling before 4xx behavior starts masking real platform issues.

issues.cause: Ingress filtering is being stressed or bypassed.
issues.title: IP Spoofing / Ingress Filtering
issues.rootReason: Abusive traffic and network instability are forcing more edge filtering decisions onto a degraded path.
issues.impactSummary: A mix of abusive traffic and edge filtering is reducing the share of requests that can be routed successfully.
issues.recommendation: Harden ingress filtering and shield the load balancer from abusive spoofed traffic earlier in the path.

issues.cause: Edge parsing and forwarding semantics are misaligned.
issues.title: HTTP Request Smuggling
issues.rootReason: Header handling drift is creating ambiguity in how the load balancer interprets and forwards requests.
issues.impactSummary: Ambiguous edge parsing is increasing reject risk and can misroute a slice of traffic.
issues.recommendation: Normalize parser behavior and strip ambiguous forwarding headers at the edge.

issues.cause: The edge is throttling traffic below the intended safe limit.
issues.title: Rate Limit False Positive
issues.rootReason: Rate limiting rules are too strict or are evaluating traffic with the wrong dimensions.
issues.impactSummary: Legitimate traffic is being throttled even though the edge is still below the intended safe request budget.
issues.recommendation: Relax the rate-limiter threshold or fix the keying strategy so legitimate traffic is not throttled prematurely.

issues.cause: Edge and backend capacity were not warmed for this burst.
issues.title: LB Pre-Warm Required
issues.rootReason: Traffic arrived faster than the LB path and backend pool could safely ramp up.
issues.impactSummary: Burst traffic is outpacing warm capacity, reducing delivery success while the pool catches up.
issues.recommendation: Pre-scale the target group or increase warm-up headroom before the next expected burst.

issues.cause: Requests are queueing faster than the backend path can drain.
issues.title: Request Queue Depth Growing
issues.rootReason: Backend service time and concurrency are insufficient for the incoming request pressure.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are spending more time waiting in queue before the backend can process them.
issues.recommendation: Reduce queueing by adding capacity, lowering burstiness, or improving backend service time.

issues.cause: The balancing strategy does not fit the traffic shape.
issues.title: Suboptimal Algorithm for Workload
issues.rootReason: Current routing policy is amplifying skew instead of smoothing it for this workload.
issues.impactSummary: The current algorithm is concentrating work unevenly and increasing hot-target pressure.
issues.recommendation: Choose a balancing strategy that better matches the request distribution and stickiness requirements.

issues.cause: Too much connection capacity is tied up in long-lived sockets.
issues.title: Excessive Keep-Alive Connections
issues.rootReason: Backend keep-alive behavior is holding onto connection slots longer than the useful request rate warrants.
issues.impactSummary: Connection headroom is being spent on long-lived sockets instead of the next burst of useful traffic.
issues.recommendation: Tighten keep-alive tuning so idle connections do not crowd out productive request work.

issues.cause: Responses are large enough to force more edge buffering.
issues.title: Large Response Buffering
issues.rootReason: Payload growth is increasing the amount of buffering the load balancer must do before forwarding responses.
issues.impactSummary: Large responses are increasing buffer pressure and delaying when data can be delivered downstream.
issues.recommendation: Reduce response size or tune buffering behavior so the edge can stream more efficiently.

issues.cause: Discovery records are churning too often.
issues.title: DNS TTL Too Low
issues.rootReason: TTL is low enough that backend address churn is adding avoidable lookup instability and stale-target risk.
issues.impactSummary: Discovery churn is increasing the chance of stale or failed backend resolution during live traffic.
issues.recommendation: Increase discovery TTL carefully so the edge is not thrashing backend resolution state.

issues.cause: Backends are returning application errors.
issues.title: Internal Server Error
issues.rootReason: The load balancer can reach the backend, but backend application logic is failing at a high rate.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are reaching the backend but too many are still failing with application-side errors.
issues.recommendation: Inspect backend application errors and stabilize the failing code path before retry pressure rises further.

issues.cause: The edge cannot accept the current request shape.
issues.title: Bad Request
issues.rootReason: Malformed or overgrown request metadata is causing the load balancer to reject requests before routing.
issues.impactSummary: Edge request validation is rejecting malformed or overgrown requests before backend delivery begins.
issues.recommendation: Normalize request headers and metadata before they reach the load balancer listener.

issues.cause: Authentication is failing before routing can complete.
issues.title: Unauthorized
issues.rootReason: The auth path in front of or behind the load balancer is rejecting requests that should authenticate successfully.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are being rejected on the auth path before the backend can serve them.
issues.recommendation: Repair the authentication dependency and validate that edge auth checks still match the backend contract.

issues.cause: The edge is denying traffic due to current policy.
issues.title: Forbidden
issues.rootReason: Load-balancer-side policy is denying requests that would otherwise route through the backend path.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are being blocked by edge policy instead of reaching the backend route.
issues.recommendation: Review the edge policy change so legitimate traffic is not blocked by mistake.

issues.cause: The edge has no matching route for this request.
issues.title: Not Found
issues.rootReason: Route conditions do not map the request to any backend target group.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are failing at the edge as unmatched routes instead of being forwarded to a backend.
issues.recommendation: Add or correct listener rules so valid traffic resolves to a backend path.

issues.cause: The request could not complete within the edge timeout budget.
issues.title: Request Timeout
issues.rootReason: A slow or unstable path is causing the edge request to time out before the response can finish.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are timing out at the edge before a full response can be delivered.
issues.recommendation: Reduce path latency or increase timeout only when the backend can safely support the longer wait.

issues.cause: Request or response payload size exceeds what the edge path can comfortably handle.
issues.title: Payload Too Large
issues.rootReason: Payload expansion is pushing the request path beyond safe buffering and delivery expectations.
issues.impactSummary: Oversized payloads are reducing successful edge delivery and increasing buffering overhead.
issues.recommendation: Shrink payloads or move large-object delivery out of the request path.

issues.cause: Request URIs are too large for the edge path.
issues.title: URI Too Long
issues.rootReason: Request metadata growth is creating path strings that are too large for listener processing expectations.
issues.impactSummary: Some requests are being rejected at the edge because the URI itself has grown beyond supported limits.
issues.recommendation: Reduce path and query-string growth before the request reaches the load balancer.

issues.cause: Traffic can route back into the load balancer path.
issues.title: Loop Detected
issues.rootReason: Topology or routing rules create a loop that can bounce traffic without reaching a terminal backend.
issues.impactSummary: Looping traffic wastes request budget and can turn a small edge issue into a wider outage.
issues.recommendation: Break the routing cycle so requests cannot loop between edge and upstream hops.

issues.cause: The edge path is configured for behavior that is not fully backed by a working route.
issues.title: Not Implemented
issues.rootReason: Control-plane drift left the listener with a route shape that no longer maps to a real backend implementation.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are reaching the edge but there is no complete backend implementation behind the active route.
issues.recommendation: Reconnect the route to a real backend implementation or remove the incomplete listener behavior.

issues.cause: The listener is not accepting the protocol version clients are sending.
issues.title: HTTP Version Not Supported
issues.rootReason: Protocol drift at the listener is rejecting requests before they can enter normal LB routing.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are being rejected at the listener because the expected protocol version does not match the traffic.
issues.recommendation: Align listener protocol policy with the clients and upstream integrations expected on this path.

issues.cause: Edge buffering and logging pressure are exhausting available storage headroom.
issues.title: Insufficient Storage
issues.rootReason: Large payload behavior and observability backpressure are consuming more edge storage than the path can safely sustain.
issues.impactSummary: Edge storage pressure is reducing the load balancer’s ability to buffer and observe requests safely.
issues.recommendation: Reduce payload and log pressure or increase the storage headroom available to the edge path.

issues.cause: The network path to the backend is timing out.
issues.title: Network Connect Timeout
issues.rootReason: Network reachability issues are preventing the load balancer from establishing a backend path before connect timeout.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are accepted at the edge but cannot complete backend network connect before the timeout budget expires.
issues.recommendation: Repair network reachability and security-path configuration so backend connects can complete normally.

issues.cause: Request headers are exceeding the edge limit.
issues.title: Headers Too Large
issues.rootReason: Cookie, auth, or metadata growth is pushing header size beyond what the load balancer allows.
issues.impactSummary: Oversized headers are being rejected before the request can enter backend routing.
issues.recommendation: Shrink request headers or raise the limit only if the upstream path can safely support it.

issues.cause: The backend connection pool is fully consumed.
issues.title: Connection Pool Exhausted
issues.rootReason: Concurrent request pressure is exhausting the full available pool instead of just approaching the limit.
issues.impactSummary: No backend connection headroom remains, so new work must queue or fail at the edge path.
issues.recommendation: Increase connection pool capacity or spread traffic across more ready backends immediately.

issues.cause: The edge idle timeout expired before the request could complete.
issues.title: Idle Timeout Exceeded
issues.rootReason: The configured idle timeout is shorter than the real request lifetime for this path.
issues.impactSummary: Long-lived requests are being cut off by the edge before the backend can finish them.
issues.recommendation: Increase idle timeout or shorten backend service time for long-lived request paths.

issues.cause: Request flow is concentrated more heavily on one target than expected.
issues.title: Uneven Request Distribution
issues.rootReason: Stickiness or hashing is sending a disproportionate share of requests to the hottest backend.
issues.impactSummary: Request flow is not spreading evenly across the pool, increasing hot-target pressure and resilience risk.
issues.recommendation: Reduce affinity skew or change the balancing strategy so request flow spreads more evenly.

issues.cause: Targets are not draining cleanly before they leave rotation.
issues.title: Deregistration Drain Timeout
issues.rootReason: Traffic continues to land on targets that are draining or still exiting the pool.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic handoff during target removal is dropping or delaying requests that should have drained cleanly.
issues.recommendation: Increase drain time or slow target removal so in-flight traffic can finish cleanly.

issues.cause: New targets are being ramped too aggressively.
issues.title: Slow Start Ramp-Up Issue
issues.rootReason: The slow-start window is not shielding fresh backends from live traffic long enough.
issues.impactSummary: Fresh targets are taking live traffic too early, which reduces delivery success during ramp-up.
issues.recommendation: Increase the slow-start window or delay live routing until the backend is fully warm.

issues.cause: The configured idle timeout is shorter than the workload needs.
issues.title: Idle Timeout Too Short
issues.rootReason: Long-lived requests or upgraded connections need a longer idle budget than the edge currently allows.
issues.impactSummary: Requests that should complete successfully are being cut off because the edge timeout is too short.
issues.recommendation: Increase idle timeout to match the real request lifetime for this path.

issues.cause: Traffic is reaching the edge from regions that should be restricted.
issues.title: Geo-Restriction Violation
issues.rootReason: Current edge policy is not cleanly enforcing the intended geographic restriction pattern.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic from outside the intended edge policy boundary is affecting load and rejection behavior.
issues.recommendation: Review geo-routing and geo-block policy so the edge enforces the intended regional boundary.

issues.cause: The slowest backend responses are stretching too far out.
issues.title: High Target Response Time P99
issues.rootReason: Tail latency on the target group is significantly worse than steady-state response time.
issues.impactSummary: The slowest backend responses are becoming much worse than the median path, degrading user experience at the tail.
issues.recommendation: Reduce backend tail latency before it turns into visible timeouts and retries.

assumptions: Seeded from the existing hand-written load_balancer specialization catalog.
