issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: JVM, Go, or Node.js GC running too frequently due to high object allocation rate. Full GC pauses ("stop-the-world") block all application threads.
issues.title: Heap Pressure / GC Storm
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-002 / Heap Pressure / GC Storm on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Periodic latency spikes correlating with GC cycles. P99 degrades while P50 stays normal.
issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: Too many threads/goroutines competing for CPU cores. OS spends more time context-switching than doing useful work. Usually caused by massively over-provisioned thread pools.
issues.title: Context Switch Storm
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-003 / Context Switch Storm on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: CPU utilization looks high but throughput is low. Latency increases non-linearly with concurrency.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: High-rate API server opens many short-lived outbound connections (to DB, cache, downstream APIs) and exhausts the 32k–60k ephemeral port range before TIME_WAIT sockets expire.
issues.title: Ephemeral Port Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: NET-001 / Ephemeral Port Exhaustion on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: New outbound connections fail with EADDRNOTAVAIL. API calls to downstream services start failing intermittently.
issues.recommendation: Raise pool capacity carefully, reuse connections more aggressively, and reduce downstream fan-out.

issues.cause: A single code path (JSON serialization, regex, cryptographic operation) consuming disproportionate CPU on every request, limiting overall throughput.
issues.title: Hot Path CPU Bottleneck
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-004 / Hot Path CPU Bottleneck on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Throughput cap lower than expected even at moderate load. Adding more instances required.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: Application accumulates memory over time without releasing it. Not visible in first hours. Eventually triggers OOMKill or degraded GC. Often caused by unclosed connections or unbounded caches.
issues.title: Memory Leak (Slow Ramp)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-003 / Memory Leak (Slow Ramp) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Memory grows monotonically per instance over hours/days; pods restart on a schedule, masking the root cause.
issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: A synchronous blocking call (file read, DNS lookup, slow DB) made inside an async event loop (Node.js, asyncio). Blocks the entire event loop, stalling all concurrent requests.
issues.title: Blocking I/O on Async Loop
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: IO-001 / Blocking I/O on Async Loop on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: One slow operation freezes all concurrent requests. Throughput collapses to near zero during the block.
issues.recommendation: Shorten the slowest dependency path, tighten timeouts intentionally, and fail fast instead of letting requests pile up.

issues.cause: During rolling deploy, the instance's shutdown hook doesn't drain in-flight requests within the termination grace period. Kubernetes SIGKILL fires, dropping active requests.
issues.title: Graceful Shutdown Timeout
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: PROC-001 / Graceful Shutdown Timeout on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Brief burst of 500 errors on every deploy. Worse with long-running synchronous calls.
issues.recommendation: Rollback quickly, validate startup/config dependencies earlier, and avoid letting broken instances join live traffic.

issues.cause: App returns non-200 on readiness probe during startup or an internal dependency check. Kubernetes removes the pod from service before it fails completely.
issues.title: Readiness Probe Failure
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: PROC-002 / Readiness Probe Failure on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Pod cycles out of rotation; traffic re-distributed to remaining healthy pods, increasing their load.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize readiness/liveness behavior and make sure probes reflect real serving health instead of transient load.

issues.cause: Liveness probe times out during GC pause or high CPU, causing Kubernetes to restart a healthy pod. Probe timeout threshold set too low.
issues.title: Liveness Probe False Positive
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: PROC-003 / Liveness Probe False Positive on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Unnecessary restarts under load; transient 503s. Can cause a cascading restart loop under sustained high traffic.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize readiness/liveness behavior and make sure probes reflect real serving health instead of transient load.

issues.cause: In a shared virtualisation environment, another tenant's VM consumes hypervisor CPU cycles, stealing them from your VM. Visible as high %steal in top/vmstat.
issues.title: CPU Steal (Noisy Neighbour)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-005 / CPU Steal (Noisy Neighbour) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Unexplained latency spikes not correlated with application metrics. Requires host migration to resolve.
issues.recommendation: Drain or replace the degraded node and confirm host-level contention is not hiding behind normal app metrics.

issues.cause: Large request payload buffered entirely in memory before processing. With high concurrency, aggregate buffer size causes memory pressure and slows GC.
issues.title: Request Body Buffer Overflow
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-004 / Request Body Buffer Overflow on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Memory usage spikes with request size × concurrency. Switch to streaming body parsing.
issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: A thread repeatedly polls a condition in a tight loop without sleeping, consuming 100% of a CPU core while waiting. Often introduced accidentally in async polling logic or broken mutex implementations.
issues.title: Spin Lock / Busy Wait
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-006 / Spin Lock / Busy Wait on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: One thread monopolises a CPU core, starving other request handlers. Visible as one core pegged at 100% while others are idle.
issues.recommendation: Increase worker capacity, isolate blocking operations, and cap concurrency on the slowest code path.

issues.cause: The kernel's TCP accept queue (backlog) fills up because the application is not calling accept() fast enough during a traffic spike. New incoming SYN packets are silently dropped.
issues.title: TCP Accept Queue Overflow
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: NET-002 / TCP Accept Queue Overflow on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Clients see connection timeouts even though the server is running. No error is logged on the server side — requires netstat/ss monitoring to detect.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around TCP Accept Queue Overflow.

issues.cause: A recursive function without a proper base case, or deeply nested call chains, exhausts the thread stack. JVM throws StackOverflowError; Node throws a maximum call stack size exceeded error.
issues.title: Stack Overflow / Deep Recursion
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-005 / Stack Overflow / Deep Recursion on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Request crashes with an unrecoverable error. In JVM, the exception may be caught at a wrong layer, masking the root cause from logs.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Stack Overflow / Deep Recursion.

issues.cause: Process opens sockets, files, or pipes faster than it closes them. Hits the ulimit (e.g., 1024 or 65535). New connection attempts fail with EMFILE ("too many open files").
issues.title: File Descriptor Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: IO-002 / File Descriptor Exhaustion on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: All outbound and inbound connections start failing. Application appears to hang. Requires immediate process restart and ulimit increase.
issues.recommendation: Raise pool capacity carefully, reuse connections more aggressively, and reduce downstream fan-out.

issues.cause: A poorly written regular expression with exponential backtracking applied to user-supplied input. A crafted string causes the regex engine to spin for seconds or minutes on a single request, pegging one CPU core.
issues.title: Regex ReDoS (Catastrophic Backtracking)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-007 / Regex ReDoS (Catastrophic Backtracking) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: One malicious or unexpected request can freeze a thread for minutes, reducing concurrency by 1 thread for its duration. Easily weaponised for DoS.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Regex ReDoS (Catastrophic Backtracking).

issues.cause: Load balancer's keep-alive timeout is longer than the application server's. The app closes the connection while the LB thinks it is still valid and tries to reuse it for the next request.
issues.title: Keep-Alive Misconfig Between LB and App
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: NET-003 / Keep-Alive Misconfig Between LB and App on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Intermittent 502s that are hard to reproduce. Happens precisely when a connection's age crosses the app-side timeout. Fix: set LB timeout slightly shorter than app timeout.
issues.recommendation: Shorten the slowest dependency path, tighten timeouts intentionally, and fail fast instead of letting requests pile up.

issues.cause: Serialization of large or deeply nested response objects consumes a disproportionate amount of CPU relative to actual business logic. Common when returning large lists without pagination.
issues.title: Serialization CPU Spike (JSON/Protobuf)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-008 / Serialization CPU Spike (JSON/Protobuf) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Throughput per core drops sharply on large-payload endpoints. CPU profiling shows serialization as the top-consuming function.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: Worker acquires a lock (DB row, Redis key, mutex) and never releases it due to an unhandled exception or missing finally block. The job neither succeeds nor fails.
issues.title: Job Hung / Deadlock
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-001 / Job Hung / Deadlock on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Queue depth grows while hung workers hold locks. Other workers stall waiting. Invisible unless you monitor lock age and job duration.
issues.recommendation: Shorten critical sections, reduce shared hot state, and partition the most contended work.

issues.cause: A malformed or unexpected message causes the worker to crash on every processing attempt. The message is re-queued each time, consuming a worker and CPU indefinitely.
issues.title: Poison Message Loop
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-002 / Poison Message Loop on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Worker crashes in a tight loop; error rate spikes; the bad message blocks queue progress behind it.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Poison Message Loop.

issues.cause: Worker killed by OOM or fatal exception while processing a job. Job may be re-queued (at-least-once delivery) causing duplicate processing, or dropped (at-most-once), causing data loss.
issues.title: Worker Crash / OOMKill Mid-Job
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-003 / Worker Crash / OOMKill Mid-Job on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Silent data inconsistency or duplicate side effects. Requires idempotency on all job handlers.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Worker Crash / OOMKill Mid-Job.

issues.cause: Job arrival rate exceeds worker processing rate. Queue depth (consumer lag) grows continuously. Caused by insufficient worker replicas, slow processing, or a sudden traffic spike.
issues.title: Queue Lag / Consumer Lag
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-004 / Queue Lag / Consumer Lag on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Background tasks delayed minutes to hours. Time-sensitive operations (email, notifications, webhooks) violate SLA.
issues.recommendation: Scale workers, split noisy queues, and keep backlog age bounded before SLA-sensitive work slips.

issues.cause: Job exceeds max execution time and is killed by the scheduler, but cleanup code (releasing locks, rolling back DB state, completing partial writes) is not run.
issues.title: Job Timeout Without Cleanup
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-005 / Job Timeout Without Cleanup on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Orphaned database locks, incomplete file uploads, or half-applied mutations left in the system.
issues.recommendation: Shorten critical sections, reduce shared hot state, and partition the most contended work.

issues.cause: Job fails repeatedly beyond the configured max retry count and moves to the Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) / dead letter topic. No automatic recovery — requires manual intervention.
issues.title: Retry Exhaustion / Dead Letter
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-006 / Retry Exhaustion / Dead Letter on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Work items silently disappear from the main queue. DLQ fills up unnoticed if monitoring is absent.
issues.recommendation: Drain the DLQ, fix the failing handler, and add alerts so retries stop hiding permanent failures.

issues.cause: Network failure between queue broker and worker causes a message to be redelivered after it was already processed. Worker is not idempotent, so the action runs twice.
issues.title: Duplicate Execution (Non-Idempotent)
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-007 / Duplicate Execution (Non-Idempotent) on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Double charges, duplicate emails, double-booked resources, or duplicate DB rows.
issues.recommendation: Add idempotency keys, extend visibility/lease windows, and make retry paths safe under redelivery.

issues.cause: Scheduled job takes longer than its interval. Next run is triggered before the previous one completes. Two instances run concurrently, causing race conditions on shared state.
issues.title: Cron Overlap / Double Trigger
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-008 / Cron Overlap / Double Trigger on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Data corruption or double-processing in batch aggregations, report generation, or cleanup jobs.
issues.recommendation: Add durable checkpoints or acknowledgements and avoid marking work complete before side effects are actually committed.

issues.cause: High-priority or large jobs monopolise all worker threads. Low-priority jobs never get scheduled. Common with a single shared worker pool and no job-type partitioning.
issues.title: Worker Starvation
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-009 / Worker Starvation on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Low-priority queues grow indefinitely; certain job types never complete.
issues.recommendation: Increase worker capacity, isolate blocking operations, and cap concurrency on the slowest code path.

issues.cause: SQS/RabbitMQ visibility timeout expires before the worker finishes processing. The message becomes visible again and is consumed by another worker while still being processed.
issues.title: Message Visibility Timeout Race
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-010 / Message Visibility Timeout Race on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Non-idempotent concurrent execution of the same job. Extend visibility timeout or heartbeat to avoid.
issues.recommendation: Add idempotency keys, extend visibility/lease windows, and make retry paths safe under redelivery.

issues.cause: Multiple scheduler instances across nodes with unsynchronised clocks trigger the same cron job at different times. NTP drift causes near-simultaneous duplicate executions.
issues.title: Scheduler Clock Skew
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-011 / Scheduler Clock Skew on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Intermittent duplicate job runs that are hard to reproduce. Mitigated by distributed lock on scheduler.
issues.recommendation: Reconcile clocks across nodes and narrow any timestamp validation windows only after time sync is stable.

issues.cause: Long-running job stops sending heartbeat signals to the orchestrator (Celery, Temporal, Sidekiq). Orchestrator assumes the worker is dead and re-schedules the job on another node.
issues.title: Missing Heartbeat / Progress Stall
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-012 / Missing Heartbeat / Progress Stall on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Wasted compute on partially completed work. Risk of two workers finishing the same job.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Missing Heartbeat / Progress Stall.

issues.cause: Worker child process terminates but the parent doesn't call wait(), leaving it as a zombie. Over time, zombie accumulation fills the process table. No new workers can be forked.
issues.title: Worker Process Zombie
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-013 / Worker Process Zombie on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Worker pool silently shrinks. Queue lag increases gradually until the host is rebooted or the supervisor is restarted.
issues.recommendation: Scale workers, split noisy queues, and keep backlog age bounded before SLA-sensitive work slips.

issues.cause: A low-priority job holds a shared resource (DB lock, mutex, semaphore) that a high-priority job needs. The high-priority job blocks waiting, effectively running at the priority of the low-priority holder.
issues.title: Priority Inversion in Queue
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-014 / Priority Inversion in Queue on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Urgent jobs stuck behind low-priority work. Entire job pipeline stalls on a seemingly unrelated low-priority task.
issues.recommendation: Shorten critical sections, reduce shared hot state, and partition the most contended work.

issues.cause: Worker concurrency set too high relative to available DB connections or memory. Each worker opens its own DB connection pool; with 20 workers × 10 connections each, the DB is flooded with 200 connections.
issues.title: Concurrency Limit Misconfiguration
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-015 / Concurrency Limit Misconfiguration on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Database connection exhaustion. All workers fail simultaneously. Reducing worker count or using a connection proxy (PgBouncer) is required.
issues.recommendation: Raise pool capacity carefully, reuse connections more aggressively, and reduce downstream fan-out.

issues.cause: Job completes and stores its result in a temporary store (Redis, Celery result backend) with a TTL. The consumer retrieves the result after the TTL has expired — the result is gone.
issues.title: Job Result Expiry Before Consumption
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-016 / Job Result Expiry Before Consumption on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Consumer receives a missing result error and must re-trigger the job, causing duplicate compute and delayed response to callers.
issues.recommendation: Add idempotency keys, extend visibility/lease windows, and make retry paths safe under redelivery.

issues.cause: Worker fetches a batch of messages and acknowledges them before fully processing. A crash after ack but before completion silently drops all messages in that batch with no redelivery.
issues.title: Batch Acknowledge Without Processing
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-017 / Batch Acknowledge Without Processing on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Silent data loss. No error is raised; queue depth drops normally. Requires post-processing audit to detect missing records.
issues.recommendation: Add durable checkpoints or acknowledgements and avoid marking work complete before side effects are actually committed.

issues.cause: New worker pods take 30–120 seconds to start (JVM warmup, model load, dependency init). During scale-out, new workers are not processing jobs during this window, creating a temporary capacity gap.
issues.title: Cold Start Latency on Worker Scale-Out
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-018 / Cold Start Latency on Worker Scale-Out on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Queue lag spikes during scale events before new workers are ready. Use pre-initialised warm pools or smaller worker startup payloads.
issues.recommendation: Pre-warm capacity or reduce startup cost so scale-out can absorb bursts before backlog accumulates.

issues.cause: Cloud provider reclaims a spot/preemptible instance mid-batch with a 2-minute warning. If the workload isn't checkpoint-aware, all progress since the last checkpoint is lost.
issues.title: Spot / Preemption Kill
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-001 / Spot / Preemption Kill on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Entire batch must restart from last checkpoint. Without checkpointing, restarts from zero — wasting hours of compute.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Spot / Preemption Kill.

issues.cause: One stage in a multi-step pipeline (extract -> transform -> load) fails. Downstream stages either proceed on bad data or are blocked, depending on pipeline design.
issues.title: Pipeline Stage Failure
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-002 / Pipeline Stage Failure on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream stages produce incorrect results silently, or the entire pipeline stalls and misses its batch window.
issues.recommendation: Add isolation boundaries between stages so one failed dependency does not poison the full pipeline.

issues.cause: Batch job attempts to load an entire dataset into memory. Works fine in test but fails in production with larger data. Instance killed by OOM before the job completes.
issues.title: Out-of-Memory on Large Dataset
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-003 / Out-of-Memory on Large Dataset on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Batch job always fails at the same scale threshold. Requires streaming/chunked processing refactor.
issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: Input data partitioned unevenly — some partitions have 100× more records than others. Workers assigned to large partitions take much longer, creating a long tail that extends total batch time.
issues.title: Data Skew / Hot Partition
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-004 / Data Skew / Hot Partition on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Batch window missed. Idle workers waiting for the one slow worker to finish before the job completes.
issues.recommendation: Rebalance partitions, spread hot tenants/keys, and avoid scaling the whole fleet to fix a single skewed shard.

issues.cause: Batch job reads/writes large files to local disk, saturating disk throughput. All other I/O on the node slows. Common during shuffle phases in Spark/MapReduce jobs.
issues.title: Disk I/O Saturation
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-005 / Disk I/O Saturation on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: I/O wait dominates CPU time. Actual processing throughput collapses. Other jobs on the same node are degraded.
issues.recommendation: Reduce spill pressure, provision faster scratch/storage capacity, and separate heavy batch I/O from latency-sensitive traffic.

issues.cause: Batch job cannot write progress checkpoint to storage (S3, GCS, NFS) due to permission error, disk full, or network issue. Job continues but all progress will be lost on restart.
issues.title: Checkpoint Write Failure
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-006 / Checkpoint Write Failure on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: If the job is later killed, it restarts from zero instead of from the checkpoint, potentially redoing hours of work.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Checkpoint Write Failure.

issues.cause: Input data format changed (new column, removed field, type changed) between batch runs. ETL pipeline fails to parse records or silently produces null/default values for missing fields.
issues.title: Schema Mismatch on Input
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-007 / Schema Mismatch on Input on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Silent data quality degradation in output. Downstream dashboards or ML models consume bad data without knowing.
issues.recommendation: Harden contract compatibility, validate inputs defensively, and avoid promoting incompatible readers/writers together.

issues.cause: Batch job takes longer than the allotted time window. Next scheduled run starts before the current one completes, causing pipeline conflicts or missed downstream dependencies.
issues.title: Batch Window Overrun
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-008 / Batch Window Overrun on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Stale data in reports; downstream pipelines waiting on this batch have to skip or fail.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Batch Window Overrun.

issues.cause: Batch dispatched across nodes with different CPU/memory/disk performance (spot + on-demand mix). Slower nodes extend total completion time and can cause partition-level failures.
issues.title: Worker Node Heterogeneity
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-009 / Worker Node Heterogeneity on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Unpredictable batch completion times. Hard to capacity-plan or SLA-guarantee.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Worker Node Heterogeneity.

issues.cause: Batch job writes incrementally to the output destination and fails mid-write. Output table/file contains a mix of new and old data, with no atomic commit boundary.
issues.title: Partial Output / Dirty Write
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-010 / Partial Output / Dirty Write on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream consumers read corrupt partial results. Requires output table isolation (write-then-swap pattern).
issues.recommendation: Add durable checkpoints or acknowledgements and avoid marking work complete before side effects are actually committed.

issues.cause: Batch job fans out to thousands of parallel sub-tasks simultaneously. Each sub-task opens DB/API connections, exhausting the connection pool even if each individual task is fast.
issues.title: Fanout Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-011 / Fanout Resource Exhaustion on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Database or API rate-limit errors at the sub-task level. Batch throughput capped far below theoretical maximum.
issues.recommendation: Raise pool capacity carefully, reuse connections more aggressively, and reduce downstream fan-out.

issues.cause: Batch job starts but the data source (S3 path, database table, Kafka topic) is missing, empty, or inaccessible due to IAM permission change or source deletion. Job either errors immediately or silently produces empty output.
issues.title: Input Source Unavailable
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-012 / Input Source Unavailable on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Empty or failed batch run. Downstream consumers receive stale or zero records. Silent if the job exits with code 0 on empty input without validation.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Input Source Unavailable.

issues.cause: Large-scale batch job reading from or writing to remote storage (S3, GCS, HDFS) saturates the instance's network interface. Other processes on the same host experience degraded network performance.
issues.title: Network Bandwidth Saturation
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-013 / Network Bandwidth Saturation on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Batch throughput plateaus at the network bandwidth limit, not CPU. Other services sharing the host degrade. Use dedicated instances or network-optimised instance types.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Network Bandwidth Saturation.

issues.cause: Spark/Flink shuffle stage spills intermediate data to local disk because it exceeds available memory. If local disk is too small, the job fails with disk full errors during the shuffle phase.
issues.title: Shuffle / Spill to Disk Overflow
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-014 / Shuffle / Spill to Disk Overflow on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Job fails mid-execution after hours of compute. Increasing executor memory or shuffle disk size is required. Hard to predict without profiling actual data sizes.
issues.recommendation: Reduce spill pressure, provision faster scratch/storage capacity, and separate heavy batch I/O from latency-sensitive traffic.

issues.cause: Batch job re-runs after a failure but appends to output instead of overwriting or using a unique run-id partition. The same records are written twice into the destination table or file.
issues.title: Idempotency Broken on Re-run
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-015 / Idempotency Broken on Re-run on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Duplicate data in analytics tables. Downstream counts and aggregations are inflated. Requires full reprocessing to clean up.
issues.recommendation: Add durable checkpoints or acknowledgements and avoid marking work complete before side effects are actually committed.

issues.cause: A downstream batch job starts before its upstream dependency has finished writing. Reads a partial dataset because orchestration dependency is misconfigured (missing wait condition or incorrect schedule offset).
issues.title: Dependency Job Not Complete
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-016 / Dependency Job Not Complete on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Batch operates on incomplete input data. Output appears valid but is missing a portion of records. Extremely hard to detect without row-count validation.
issues.recommendation: Add isolation boundaries between stages so one failed dependency does not poison the full pipeline.

issues.cause: Batch job runs far longer than expected (due to data skew, hot partitions, or under-provisioned workers) on a pay-per-second cloud resource. No cost guardrails or max-duration limits are set.
issues.title: Cost Overrun on Long-Running Job
issues.rootReason: The offline batch path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: BATCH-017 / Cost Overrun on Long-Running Job on App Batch Compute.
issues.impactSummary: Unexpectedly high compute bill. Common on on-demand or serverless batch (AWS Glue, Dataproc). Add job duration alerts and auto-termination policies.
issues.recommendation: Add duration/cost guardrails and stop long-running jobs before they silently turn into runaway spend.

issues.cause: Third-party API becomes slow. Threads waiting for the response accumulate, exhausting the thread pool. Your service becomes unavailable not due to internal issues but due to an external dependency stall.
issues.title: External API Timeout Cascade
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-001 / External API Timeout Cascade on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Your entire service becomes unresponsive due to an external dependency. Requires circuit breaker + bulkhead isolation.
issues.recommendation: Shorten the slowest dependency path, tighten timeouts intentionally, and fail fast instead of letting requests pile up.

issues.cause: Your service exceeds the API quota of an external provider. Often due to a spike in user activity, a batch operation calling the external API in a tight loop, or missing rate-limit-aware client logic.
issues.title: External Rate Limit Hit (429)
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-002 / External Rate Limit Hit (429) on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: External calls fail; your service must queue, backoff, and retry — or degrade gracefully. No quick fix without quota increase.
issues.recommendation: Back off on provider quotas, throttle callers deliberately, and buffer work instead of hammering the dependency.

issues.cause: Circuit breaker detects sustained failures from an external dependency and opens the circuit, fast-failing all subsequent calls without attempting them. State is open until half-open probe succeeds.
issues.title: Circuit Breaker Open
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-003 / Circuit Breaker Open on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: All requests to the downstream service fail immediately. Requires a fallback path or user-facing degraded state.
issues.recommendation: Add a degraded fallback and keep the breaker narrow enough that one failing dependency does not stall the whole service.

issues.cause: Your service cannot deliver a webhook to a partner because their endpoint is down, returning non-2xx, or timing out. Retry with exponential backoff; if all retries exhaust, the event is lost or DLQ'd.
issues.title: Webhook Delivery Failure
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-004 / Webhook Delivery Failure on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Partner does not receive events; data sync breaks silently. Partner may call with inconsistent state complaints days later.
issues.recommendation: Scale delivery workers, back off slow partner endpoints, and isolate retries from fresh event delivery.

issues.cause: External hostname cannot be resolved — DNS server unreachable, NXDOMAIN for a decommissioned service, or TTL-expired record not yet refreshed. Causes immediate connection failure.
issues.title: DNS Resolution Failure
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-005 / DNS Resolution Failure on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: All calls to the external service fail with a socket error rather than an HTTP error. Harder to diagnose without DNS-level observability.
issues.recommendation: Verify DNS/egress configuration, cache healthy resolutions cautiously, and alert on resolver failure before the app times out.

issues.cause: The external API's certificate has expired, its hostname doesn't match, or it presents an untrusted CA. Your HTTP client refuses to connect if strict TLS validation is enabled.
issues.title: TLS Certificate Error on Upstream
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-006 / TLS Certificate Error on Upstream on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: All calls to the external service fail with TLS errors. May require disabling strict validation as a workaround — creates security risk.
issues.recommendation: Renew credentials/certificates ahead of expiry and validate trust chains during rollout, not only after failure.

issues.cause: External API goes down. All clients retry simultaneously without jitter. The thundering herd of retries hammers the recovering external service, delaying its recovery.
issues.title: Retry Storm on External Failure
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-007 / Retry Storm on External Failure on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: External service stays down longer due to your own retry traffic. Exponential backoff + full jitter is required.
issues.recommendation: Use capped retries with full jitter and prefer queues or fallbacks over synchronized retry fan-out.

issues.cause: External API silently changes its response shape — renamed field, changed type, dropped required key. Deserialization in your code throws an exception or produces null values.
issues.title: Response Schema Change (Breaking)
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-008 / Response Schema Change (Breaking) on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Integration breaks silently or with serialization errors. No SLA guarantee from the provider. Requires defensive deserialization.
issues.recommendation: Harden contract compatibility, validate inputs defensively, and avoid promoting incompatible readers/writers together.

issues.cause: OAuth token, API key, or service account credential has expired or been rotated by the external provider. All calls fail with 401 Unauthorized until credential is refreshed and redeployed.
issues.title: Credentials / API Key Expired
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-009 / Credentials / API Key Expired on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Hard-stop failure for all operations requiring that external service. Requires credential rotation procedure and automation.
issues.recommendation: Automate rotation validation, keep credential rollout atomic, and alert on 401/403 bursts immediately.

issues.cause: Calling a mutating external API (payment, order creation) without an idempotency key. On retry after a network failure, the operation is executed twice — charging the customer twice.
issues.title: Idempotency Key Missing
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-010 / Idempotency Key Missing on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Financial or data integrity issues. Extremely difficult to detect without reconciliation. Requires idempotency key on all mutating calls.
issues.recommendation: Add idempotency keys, extend visibility/lease windows, and make retry paths safe under redelivery.

issues.cause: HTTP client connection pool to external service is too small. Under load, requests queue waiting for a connection slot. Adds latency proportional to queue wait time.
issues.title: Connection Pool Starvation (Downstream)
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-011 / Connection Pool Starvation (Downstream) on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream latency bleeds into your response time. Looks like your service is slow when the bottleneck is the connection pool size.
issues.recommendation: Raise pool capacity carefully, reuse connections more aggressively, and reduce downstream fan-out.

issues.cause: Inbound webhook from external provider fails HMAC/signature verification — either due to a clock skew beyond tolerance, key mismatch after rotation, or a replay attack being blocked.
issues.title: Webhook Signature Verification Fail
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-012 / Webhook Signature Verification Fail on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Legitimate webhooks rejected; partner events missed. Clock sync and key rotation procedures must be rock-solid.
issues.recommendation: Reconcile clocks across nodes and narrow any timestamp validation windows only after time sync is stable.

issues.cause: External API responses cached aggressively to reduce quota usage. External data changes but your cache returns the stale version past its usefulness, causing incorrect behaviour.
issues.title: Response Caching Staleness
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-013 / Response Caching Staleness on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Users see stale external data. Requires cache invalidation strategy aligned with data freshness requirements.
issues.recommendation: Align freshness expectations with cache strategy and avoid serving long-lived cached external data without revalidation.

issues.cause: External API endpoint is deprecated and returns 410 Gone or redirects to a new URL. If your client doesn't handle 410, it may retry indefinitely or return incorrect data from the redirect.
issues.title: Vendor API Deprecation (Silent 410)
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-014 / Vendor API Deprecation (Silent 410) on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Integration silently breaks. May go undetected if the 410 response has a plausible-looking body. Monitor all non-2xx responses from external APIs.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Vendor API Deprecation (Silent 410).

issues.cause: Multiple concurrent threads detect an expired OAuth token and all simultaneously attempt to refresh it. Each thread obtains a different new token; earlier tokens are invalidated by the last refresh, causing 401s on concurrent requests.
issues.title: OAuth Token Race Condition
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-015 / OAuth Token Race Condition on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Burst of 401 errors under high concurrency. Requires a single-flight token refresh with a lock so only one goroutine/thread refreshes at a time.
issues.recommendation: Automate rotation validation, keep credential rollout atomic, and alert on 401/403 bursts immediately.

issues.cause: External integration is expected to deliver data or events within a contractual time window (e.g., payment settlements, order confirmations). A compute or network issue causes the delivery to fall outside the window.
issues.title: Partner SLA Window Missed
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-016 / Partner SLA Window Missed on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: SLA breach with financial or legal consequences. Requires latency alerting on integration pipelines, not just error-rate alerting.
issues.recommendation: Shorten the slowest dependency path, tighten timeouts intentionally, and fail fast instead of letting requests pile up.

issues.cause: External API responds with a 301/302 that redirects back to itself or into a cycle. HTTP client follows up to its redirect limit (e.g., 10) then throws an error, wasting time and network round-trips.
issues.title: Redirect Loop on External Endpoint
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-017 / Redirect Loop on External Endpoint on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Each call wastes 10× the network round-trips before failing. High latency and connection count on calls to that endpoint.
issues.recommendation: Shorten the slowest dependency path, tighten timeouts intentionally, and fail fast instead of letting requests pile up.

issues.cause: Your compute runs in a private subnet with restrictive egress rules. A newly added external API domain or IP range is not whitelisted. Calls fail at the network layer with ECONNREFUSED or timeout, not an HTTP error.
issues.title: Proxy / Egress Firewall Block
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-018 / Proxy / Egress Firewall Block on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Integration fails silently with a network error that looks like an outage on the external side. Requires egress firewall audit and domain-based allow-listing.
issues.recommendation: Audit egress and service-to-service policy boundaries before assuming the dependency itself is down.

issues.cause: Integration relies on polling an external API for updates at a fixed interval. Events occur faster than the polling interval, causing some events to be missed between polls or overwritten by newer state.
issues.title: Polling Inefficiency (Missed Events)
issues.rootReason: The external dependency path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: EXT-019 / Polling Inefficiency (Missed Events) on App External Integration.
issues.impactSummary: Events silently dropped between poll cycles. Requires switching to webhook-based push delivery or shortening the poll interval with proper backoff.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Polling Inefficiency (Missed Events).

issues.cause: Kubernetes node stops reporting to the control plane — kubelet crash, network partition, or host OS freeze. All pods on the node are evicted after the node timeout and rescheduled elsewhere.
issues.title: Node NotReady
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-001 / Node NotReady on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Any running profile loses all pods on that node simultaneously. Increases load on remaining nodes during reschedule window.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Node NotReady.

issues.cause: Container registry unreachable, auth token expired, or the image tag does not exist. Pods stay in ImagePullBackOff or ErrImagePull state — new or restarting pods cannot start.
issues.title: Image Pull Failure
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-002 / Image Pull Failure on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Scale-out events and restarts fail silently. Traffic continues routing to existing healthy pods until they run out.
issues.recommendation: Rollback quickly, validate startup/config dependencies earlier, and avoid letting broken instances join live traffic.

issues.cause: Node runs low on memory or disk. Kubelet evicts lower-priority pods to free resources for higher-priority ones. Pods without PodDisruptionBudget protection are evicted without warning.
issues.title: Pod Eviction (Resource Pressure)
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-003 / Pod Eviction (Resource Pressure) on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Low-priority profiles (Batch, Background Jobs) evicted first. High-priority (Sync API) pods may still be affected if eviction is severe enough.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Pod Eviction (Resource Pressure).

issues.cause: Pod references a ConfigMap or Secret that doesn't exist or was deleted. Pod fails to start or crashes on first read of the missing configuration at runtime.
issues.title: ConfigMap / Secret Not Found
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-004 / ConfigMap / Secret Not Found on Default.
issues.impactSummary: All profiles dependent on that config fail to start. New deployments and pod restarts fail until the missing resource is restored.
issues.recommendation: Automate rotation validation, keep credential rollout atomic, and alert on 401/403 bursts immediately.

issues.cause: HPA takes 1–3 minutes to detect high CPU/memory and provision new pods. Traffic spike hits before scale-out completes, overloading existing pods during the scale lag window.
issues.title: Horizontal Autoscaler Lag
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-005 / Horizontal Autoscaler Lag on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Brief overload period on every traffic spike. High-priority profiles suffer most; batch/background profiles tolerate it better.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: Persistent Volume Claim cannot be mounted — storage backend unavailable, PVC stuck in Terminating, or wrong access mode (ReadWriteOnce mounted by multiple pods in different nodes).
issues.title: PVC / Storage Mount Failure
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-006 / PVC / Storage Mount Failure on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Any profile writing to persistent storage (logs, checkpoints, uploads) fails to start or crashes at first write.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around PVC / Storage Mount Failure.

issues.cause: CPU/memory requests set too low relative to actual usage. Pod scheduled on a node without enough headroom; throttled immediately under load. Limits set too high; node over-provisioned.
issues.title: Resource Request/Limit Mismatch
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-007 / Resource Request/Limit Mismatch on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Scheduling issues, CPU throttling, or OOM kills depending on which side is misconfigured. Affects all profiles but severity depends on workload type.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: No PDB configured. During cluster upgrade or node drain, Kubernetes may simultaneously evict all pods of a deployment, causing full downtime instead of rolling disruption.
issues.title: Missing PodDisruptionBudget
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-008 / Missing PodDisruptionBudget on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Any maintenance window or upgrade can cause zero-instance availability for any profile. PDB with minAvailable=1 prevents this.
issues.recommendation: Add redundancy or autoscaled failover capacity so maintenance and drains cannot take the whole path down.

issues.cause: Kubernetes etcd is overloaded by excessive API calls (controller loops, too many watchers, large CRD objects). API server becomes slow or unavailable; scheduler and controller-manager stop working.
issues.title: etcd Overload / Control Plane Lag
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-009 / etcd Overload / Control Plane Lag on Default.
issues.impactSummary: New pods cannot be scheduled; deployments hang; HPA stops scaling. Existing running pods are unaffected but no changes can be applied until etcd recovers.
issues.recommendation: Rollback quickly, validate startup/config dependencies earlier, and avoid letting broken instances join live traffic.

issues.cause: A new or modified Kubernetes NetworkPolicy inadvertently blocks traffic between namespaces, or between a service and its dependencies. Traffic that previously worked silently drops after the policy is applied.
issues.title: Network Policy Blocking Internal Traffic
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-010 / Network Policy Blocking Internal Traffic on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Service-to-service calls fail with connection refused or timeout. All profiles relying on internal service mesh communication are affected. Policy audit required.
issues.recommendation: Audit egress and service-to-service policy boundaries before assuming the dependency itself is down.

issues.cause: Pod's Kubernetes service account lacks the RBAC permission or IAM role binding (IRSA on EKS, Workload Identity on GKE) to access a cloud resource. Calls to S3, Secrets Manager, or other cloud APIs fail with 403.
issues.title: Service Account / IRSA Permission Error
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-011 / Service Account / IRSA Permission Error on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Any profile accessing cloud services (reading secrets, writing to S3, calling Bedrock/OpenAI, etc.) fails silently or loudly with access denied errors.
issues.recommendation: Automate rotation validation, keep credential rollout atomic, and alert on 401/403 bursts immediately.

issues.cause: CoreDNS pods are overloaded by excessive DNS lookups from the cluster (e.g., every HTTP call triggers a fresh DNS lookup). CoreDNS becomes a bottleneck; lookups queue up and time out.
issues.title: DNS Cache Poisoning / CoreDNS Overload
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-012 / DNS Cache Poisoning / CoreDNS Overload on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Service discovery breaks intermittently. Calls to internal and external services fail with DNS timeout errors. Mitigated by enabling ndots tuning and in-pod DNS caching.
issues.recommendation: Verify DNS/egress configuration, cache healthy resolutions cautiously, and alert on resolver failure before the app times out.

issues.cause: Application emitting extremely high log volumes saturates the log shipper (Fluentd, Fluent Bit). Log buffer fills; new log entries are dropped or the shipper applies backpressure, causing the application's write to /dev/log to block.
issues.title: Log Volume / Fluentd Backpressure
issues.rootReason: The runtime is matching a cross-profile infrastructure issue pattern. Signature: INFRA-013 / Log Volume / Fluentd Backpressure on Default.
issues.impactSummary: Logs are silently dropped during high-traffic periods — exactly when you need them most for debugging. In extreme cases, application throughput degrades due to blocked log writes.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Log Volume / Fluentd Backpressure.

assumptions: Seeded from the existing hand-written compute specialization catalog.
reviewWarnings: Review component-level applicability before shipping runtime behavior changes.

issues.cause: The rate of messages being produced into the queue is consistently higher than the rate at which consumers can process them, leading to an ever-increasing backlog or message rejection.
issues.title: Queue Overflow / Backlog Growth
issues.rootReason: Insufficient consumer capacity, slow consumer processing, or a sudden spike in upstream message production without corresponding elastic scaling of consumers.
issues.impactSummary: New messages may be rejected, leading to data loss for producers. The queue can become a bottleneck, causing upstream services to experience higher latency or errors. Overall system throughput is reduced.
issues.recommendation: Increase the number of consumer instances (scale out), optimize consumer application code for better throughput, or implement backpressure mechanisms on producers to slow down message ingestion. Evaluate whether the queue's configured capacity is sufficient.

issues.cause: Messages are residing in the queue for an extended period, exceeding defined latency tolerance. This typically indicates processing delays.
issues.title: High Message Age
issues.rootReason: Consumers are either slow, stalled, or not processing messages fast enough, leading to messages 'stalling' in the queue before reaching their processing destination.
issues.impactSummary: Time-sensitive operations may fail or become irrelevant. Data staleness increases, impacting downstream systems that rely on fresh data. Violations of Service Level Objectives (SLOs) for message processing latency.
issues.recommendation: Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks within consumer applications. Increase consumer parallelism, tune batch sizes, or investigate external dependencies of consumers. Ensure consumers are healthy and actively pulling messages.

issues.cause: The total number of unacknowledged messages that consumers are behind in processing is growing, signifying a significant delay between message production and consumption.
issues.title: Significant Consumer Lag
issues.rootReason: Persistent under-provisioning of consumers, consumer application errors preventing processing, or external service dependencies impacting consumer throughput.
issues.impactSummary: Real-time processing guarantees are compromised. Downstream systems receive delayed data, potentially leading to inconsistencies. The queue itself might accumulate a large backlog, exacerbating related issues like 'Queue Overflow'.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the consumer fleet to match the message ingestion rate. Optimize consumer code for efficiency. Implement robust error handling and retry mechanisms to prevent consumers from getting stuck on specific messages. Investigate consumer health and resource utilization.

issues.cause: Messages that failed multiple processing attempts are being routed to the Dead Letter Queue, indicating persistent processing failures.
issues.title: Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Growth
issues.rootReason: Poison messages (malformed or invalid messages), persistent bugs in consumer application logic, or continuous unavailability/errors in downstream services that consumers depend on.
issues.impactSummary: Critical messages may be lost or stuck, preventing business processes from completing. Requires manual intervention to diagnose and resolve. Potentially indicates a systemic issue in message processing or data quality.
issues.recommendation: Inspect messages in the DLQ to identify common failure patterns. Debug and fix consumer code or data issues. Address external dependencies causing failures. Implement mechanisms to reprocess or discard DLQ messages after resolution.

issues.cause: A significant number of messages are entering retry loops, causing repeated processing attempts that may overload consumers and the queue itself.
issues.title: Message Retry Storm
issues.rootReason: Transient errors in downstream services, misconfigured retry policies (e.g., overly aggressive retries, lack of exponential backoff), or a widespread consumer bug causing repeated failures that trigger retries.
issues.impactSummary: Increased load on consumers and the queue due to re-processing the same messages. May lead to cascading failures, resource exhaustion, or reduced throughput for new, valid messages. Service latency can increase significantly.
issues.recommendation: Implement exponential backoff and jitter for retries. Add circuit breakers to consumers when interacting with unreliable downstream dependencies. Review and adjust retry limits and move failing messages to a DLQ more quickly if problems persist. Investigate the root cause of the transient failures.

assumptions: The message queue component is used for asynchronous processing between services.
Metrics for queue depth, message age, consumer lag, and DLQ volume are available.
Consumers are typically external services or applications that connect to the queue.

issues.cause: Container or VM exceeds its CPU quota/limit. Kernel throttles the process, adding latency to every syscall and computation during the burst period.
issues.title: CPU Throttling
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-001 / CPU Throttling on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: P99 latency spikes; users experience slow API responses even though the app itself is healthy.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: All worker threads are busy handling in-flight requests. New requests queue up in the OS accept queue or the application queue until a thread is free.
issues.title: Thread Pool Saturation
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-002 / Thread Pool Saturation on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Synchronous callers time out waiting for a thread. Cascading 503s upstream.
issues.recommendation: Increase worker capacity, isolate blocking operations, and cap concurrency on the slowest code path.

issues.cause: Container memory usage exceeds its limit. The Linux OOM killer terminates the process immediately with no warning. All in-flight requests are lost.
issues.title: OOM Kill (OOMKilled)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-001 / OOM Kill (OOMKilled) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Sudden 502/503 burst. Requests mid-execution are dropped with no error response to the client.
issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: JVM, Go, or Node.js GC running too frequently due to high object allocation rate. Full GC pauses ("stop-the-world") block all application threads.
issues.title: Heap Pressure / GC Storm
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-002 / Heap Pressure / GC Storm on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Periodic latency spikes correlating with GC cycles. P99 degrades while P50 stays normal.
issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: Too many threads/goroutines competing for CPU cores. OS spends more time context-switching than doing useful work. Usually caused by massively over-provisioned thread pools.
issues.title: Context Switch Storm
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-003 / Context Switch Storm on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: CPU utilization looks high but throughput is low. Latency increases non-linearly with concurrency.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: High-rate API server opens many short-lived outbound connections (to DB, cache, downstream APIs) and exhausts the 32k–60k ephemeral port range before TIME_WAIT sockets expire.
issues.title: Ephemeral Port Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: NET-001 / Ephemeral Port Exhaustion on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: New outbound connections fail with EADDRNOTAVAIL. API calls to downstream services start failing intermittently.
issues.recommendation: Raise pool capacity carefully, reuse connections more aggressively, and reduce downstream fan-out.

issues.cause: A single code path (JSON serialization, regex, cryptographic operation) consuming disproportionate CPU on every request, limiting overall throughput.
issues.title: Hot Path CPU Bottleneck
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-004 / Hot Path CPU Bottleneck on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Throughput cap lower than expected even at moderate load. Adding more instances required.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: Application accumulates memory over time without releasing it. Not visible in first hours. Eventually triggers OOMKill or degraded GC. Often caused by unclosed connections or unbounded caches.
issues.title: Memory Leak (Slow Ramp)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-003 / Memory Leak (Slow Ramp) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Memory grows monotonically per instance over hours/days; pods restart on a schedule, masking the root cause.
issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: A synchronous blocking call (file read, DNS lookup, slow DB) made inside an async event loop (Node.js, asyncio). Blocks the entire event loop, stalling all concurrent requests.
issues.title: Blocking I/O on Async Loop
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: IO-001 / Blocking I/O on Async Loop on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: One slow operation freezes all concurrent requests. Throughput collapses to near zero during the block.
issues.recommendation: Shorten the slowest dependency path, tighten timeouts intentionally, and fail fast instead of letting requests pile up.

issues.cause: During rolling deploy, the instance's shutdown hook doesn't drain in-flight requests within the termination grace period. Kubernetes SIGKILL fires, dropping active requests.
issues.title: Graceful Shutdown Timeout
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: PROC-001 / Graceful Shutdown Timeout on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Brief burst of 500 errors on every deploy. Worse with long-running synchronous calls.
issues.recommendation: Rollback quickly, validate startup/config dependencies earlier, and avoid letting broken instances join live traffic.

issues.cause: App returns non-200 on readiness probe during startup or an internal dependency check. Kubernetes removes the pod from service before it fails completely.
issues.title: Readiness Probe Failure
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: PROC-002 / Readiness Probe Failure on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Pod cycles out of rotation; traffic re-distributed to remaining healthy pods, increasing their load.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize readiness/liveness behavior and make sure probes reflect real serving health instead of transient load.

issues.cause: Liveness probe times out during GC pause or high CPU, causing Kubernetes to restart a healthy pod. Probe timeout threshold set too low.
issues.title: Liveness Probe False Positive
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: PROC-003 / Liveness Probe False Positive on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Unnecessary restarts under load; transient 503s. Can cause a cascading restart loop under sustained high traffic.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize readiness/liveness behavior and make sure probes reflect real serving health instead of transient load.

issues.cause: In a shared virtualisation environment, another tenant's VM consumes hypervisor CPU cycles, stealing them from your VM. Visible as high %steal in top/vmstat.
issues.title: CPU Steal (Noisy Neighbour)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-005 / CPU Steal (Noisy Neighbour) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Unexplained latency spikes not correlated with application metrics. Requires host migration to resolve.
issues.recommendation: Drain or replace the degraded node and confirm host-level contention is not hiding behind normal app metrics.

issues.cause: Large request payload buffered entirely in memory before processing. With high concurrency, aggregate buffer size causes memory pressure and slows GC.
issues.title: Request Body Buffer Overflow
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-004 / Request Body Buffer Overflow on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Memory usage spikes with request size × concurrency. Switch to streaming body parsing.
issues.recommendation: Lower per-request memory, stream large payloads, and add guardrails before heap pressure turns into restarts.

issues.cause: A thread repeatedly polls a condition in a tight loop without sleeping, consuming 100% of a CPU core while waiting. Often introduced accidentally in async polling logic or broken mutex implementations.
issues.title: Spin Lock / Busy Wait
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-006 / Spin Lock / Busy Wait on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: One thread monopolises a CPU core, starving other request handlers. Visible as one core pegged at 100% while others are idle.
issues.recommendation: Increase worker capacity, isolate blocking operations, and cap concurrency on the slowest code path.

issues.cause: The kernel's TCP accept queue (backlog) fills up because the application is not calling accept() fast enough during a traffic spike. New incoming SYN packets are silently dropped.
issues.title: TCP Accept Queue Overflow
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: NET-002 / TCP Accept Queue Overflow on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Clients see connection timeouts even though the server is running. No error is logged on the server side — requires netstat/ss monitoring to detect.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around TCP Accept Queue Overflow.

issues.cause: A recursive function without a proper base case, or deeply nested call chains, exhausts the thread stack. JVM throws StackOverflowError; Node throws a maximum call stack size exceeded error.
issues.title: Stack Overflow / Deep Recursion
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: MEM-005 / Stack Overflow / Deep Recursion on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Request crashes with an unrecoverable error. In JVM, the exception may be caught at a wrong layer, masking the root cause from logs.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Stack Overflow / Deep Recursion.

issues.cause: Process opens sockets, files, or pipes faster than it closes them. Hits the ulimit (e.g., 1024 or 65535). New connection attempts fail with EMFILE ("too many open files").
issues.title: File Descriptor Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: IO-002 / File Descriptor Exhaustion on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: All outbound and inbound connections start failing. Application appears to hang. Requires immediate process restart and ulimit increase.
issues.recommendation: Raise pool capacity carefully, reuse connections more aggressively, and reduce downstream fan-out.

issues.cause: A poorly written regular expression with exponential backtracking applied to user-supplied input. A crafted string causes the regex engine to spin for seconds or minutes on a single request, pegging one CPU core.
issues.title: Regex ReDoS (Catastrophic Backtracking)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-007 / Regex ReDoS (Catastrophic Backtracking) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: One malicious or unexpected request can freeze a thread for minutes, reducing concurrency by 1 thread for its duration. Easily weaponised for DoS.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Regex ReDoS (Catastrophic Backtracking).

issues.cause: Load balancer's keep-alive timeout is longer than the application server's. The app closes the connection while the LB thinks it is still valid and tries to reuse it for the next request.
issues.title: Keep-Alive Misconfig Between LB and App
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: NET-003 / Keep-Alive Misconfig Between LB and App on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Intermittent 502s that are hard to reproduce. Happens precisely when a connection's age crosses the app-side timeout. Fix: set LB timeout slightly shorter than app timeout.
issues.recommendation: Shorten the slowest dependency path, tighten timeouts intentionally, and fail fast instead of letting requests pile up.

issues.cause: Serialization of large or deeply nested response objects consumes a disproportionate amount of CPU relative to actual business logic. Common when returning large lists without pagination.
issues.title: Serialization CPU Spike (JSON/Protobuf)
issues.rootReason: The synchronous request path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: CPU-008 / Serialization CPU Spike (JSON/Protobuf) on App Synchronous API.
issues.impactSummary: Throughput per core drops sharply on large-payload endpoints. CPU profiling shows serialization as the top-consuming function.
issues.recommendation: Reduce hot paths, right-size CPU limits, and add horizontal headroom before the next burst.

issues.cause: Worker acquires a lock (DB row, Redis key, mutex) and never releases it due to an unhandled exception or missing finally block. The job neither succeeds nor fails.
issues.title: Job Hung / Deadlock
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-001 / Job Hung / Deadlock on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Queue depth grows while hung workers hold locks. Other workers stall waiting. Invisible unless you monitor lock age and job duration.
issues.recommendation: Shorten critical sections, reduce shared hot state, and partition the most contended work.

issues.cause: A malformed or unexpected message causes the worker to crash on every processing attempt. The message is re-queued each time, consuming a worker and CPU indefinitely.
issues.title: Poison Message Loop
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-002 / Poison Message Loop on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Worker crashes in a tight loop; error rate spikes; the bad message blocks queue progress behind it.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Poison Message Loop.

issues.cause: Worker killed by OOM or fatal exception while processing a job. Job may be re-queued (at-least-once delivery) causing duplicate processing, or dropped (at-most-once), causing data loss.
issues.title: Worker Crash / OOMKill Mid-Job
issues.rootReason: The async worker path is matching the compute reference for this workload. Signature: JOB-003 / Worker Crash / OOMKill Mid-Job on App Background Jobs.
issues.impactSummary: Silent data inconsistency or duplicate side effects. Requires idempotency on all job handlers.
issues.recommendation: Stabilize the failing compute path, reduce blast radius, and add targeted observability around Worker Crash / OOMKill Mid-Job.
